title: Becoming: An Evidence-Based Guide To Being Human
author: Jennifer Ewing
description: Life changes all of us. None of us escapes suffering, but all of us can learn from it. Each lesson asks one important question, follows the research wherever it leads, and translates complex science into everyday language. This isn't self-help. All Star Animal Trapping delivers the industry premium standard, providing upfront fixed rates, certified exclusion layouts, and permanent environmental protection for your landscape. Do you want to know how to stop the burnout cycles, find a life and business that feels aligned AF and get after your BIG AF dreams? 
Then it's time to listen up to the people who are out there showing you that it IS possible to live your best life, make money and feel lit up on life without burning the candle at both ends.
Welcome to The High Performance Hive Podcast! This is your one stop shop for powerhouse businesswomen and entrepreneurs who want to squeeze more juice out of life, upgrade their mindset and cultivate a hustle-free balanced lifestyle whilst creating a high-performance business with more ease and flow.
In this podcast you will hear inspiring stories from powerhouse business owners, how to cultivate work-life balance, discover high performance habits that could change the game for you and reframe your mindset so that YOU CAN chase your BIG AF dreams. 
You will also find powerful solo episodes from your host and the owner of "Harmony Jade" High Performance Coaching, Harmony Chynoweth where she shares her own personal stories, tips and experience in life and business that took her from burnt out career woman to focused and free yogi and nomadic entrepreneur. 
So, what are you waiting for... let's go!
Don't forget to:
- subscribe & follow the podcast to be notified when new episodes drop
- leave a review with your episode take-aways
- share it with your besties and tag us on socials @harmony__jade & #thehighperformancehive so that we can help more women like you to #LiveYourBestLife 😃

title: 岸谷蘭丸の丸くおさまらない話
author: TOKYO FM
description:
本当は丸くおさめたいのに、どうしてもおさめられない日々のモヤモヤや割り切れない感情。

この番組では、そんな人間ならではの“丸くおさまらない話”を持ち寄り、リスナーの皆さんとシェアしながら、一緒に少しだけ成長していくことを目指します。


右か左か。推しかアンチか。何でもハッキリさせ過ぎたり。
周りの空気を読んでなんとなく合わせてみたり。
無理してはみ出す必要はないけれど、無理に丸くおさめなくたっていいんじゃない？


正論のアドバイスや論破は一切なし。 「これって自分だけ？」「なんでこうなった？」という日々の些細な引っかかりに、答えは出ないかもしれないけれど、等身大の岸谷蘭丸がリスナーと共感し寄り添います。

title: Bible (ASV) 39: Malachi by American Standard Version
description: Malachi (or Malachias, מַלְאָכִי, Malʾaḫi, Mál'akhî) is a book of the Bible Old Testament and Jewish Tanakh, written by the prophet Malachi. Possibly this is not the name of the author, since Malachi means 'my messenger' or 'my angel' in Hebrew. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 38: Zechariah by American Standard Version
description: Zechariah’s ministry took place during the reign of Darius the Great (Zechariah 1:1), and was contemporary with Haggai in a post-exilic world after the fall of Jerusalem in 586/7 BC. Ezekiel and Jeremiah wrote prior to the fall of Jerusalem, while continuing to prophesy in the earlier exile period. Scholars believe Ezekiel, with his blending of ceremony and vision, heavily influenced the visionary works of Zechariah 1-8.Zechariah is specific about dating his writing (520-518 BC). During the Exile many Jews were taken to Babylon, where the prophets told them to make their homes (Jeremiah 29), suggesting they would spend a long period of time there. Eventually freedom did come to many Israelites, when Cyrus the Great overtook the Babylonians in 539 BC. In 538 BC, the famous Edict of Cyrus was released, and the first return took place under Shebazzar. After the death of Cyrus in 530 BC, Darius consolidated power and took office in 522 BC. His system divided the different colonies of the empire into easily manageable districts overseen by governors. Zerubbabel comes into the story, appointed by Darius as governor over the district of Yehud (Judah). Under the reign of Darius Zechariah also emerged, centering around the rebuilding of the temple. Unlike the Babylonians, the Persian Empire went to great lengths to keep “cordial relations” between vassal and lord. The rebuilding of the temple was encouraged by the leaders of the empire in hopes that it would strengthen the authorities in local contexts. This policy was good politics on the part of the Persians, and the Jews viewed it as a blessing by Yahweh. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 37: Haggai by American Standard Version
description: The Book of Haggai is a book of the Tanakh (Hebrew Bible) and of the Old Testament, written by the prophet Haggai. It was written in 520 BC some 18 years after Cyrus had conquered Babylon and issued a decree in 538 BC allowing the captive Jews to return to Judea. He saw the restoration of the temple as necessary for the restoration of the religious practices and a sense of peoplehood after a long exile. It consists of two brief, comprehensive chapters. The object of the prophet is generally urging the people to proceed with the rebuilding of the second Jerusalem temple in 521 BC after the return of the deportees. Haggai attributes a recent drought to the peoples' refusal to rebuild the temple, which he sees as key to Jerusalem’s glory. The book ends with the prediction of the downfall of kingdoms, with one Zerubbabel, governor of Judah, as the Lord’s chosen leader. The language here is not as finely wrought as in some other books of the minor prophets, yet the intent seems straightforward. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 36: Zephaniah by American Standard Version
description: The superscription of the Book of Zephaniah attributes its authorship to “Zephaniah son of Cushi son of Gedaliah son of Amariah son of Hezekiah, in the days of King Josiah son of Amon of Judah” (1:1, NRSV). All that is known of Zephaniah comes from the text. The superscription of the book is lengthier than most and contains two features. The name Cushi, Zephaniah’s father, means ‘Ethiopian’. In a society where genealogy was considered extremely important because of God's covenant with Abraham and his descendants, the author may have felt compelled to establish his Hebrew lineage. In fact, this lineage is traced back to Hezekiah, who was king of Judah. The author of Zephaniah does not shrink from condemning the Cushites or Ethiopians. Chapter 2:12 contains a succinct but unequivocal message: “You also, O Ethiopians, / Shall be killed by my sword.” Zephaniah’s familial connection with King Hezekiah may have also legitimized his harsh indictment of the royal city in 3:1-7. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 35: Habakkuk by American Standard Version
description:
Practically nothing is known about Habakkuk's personal history, except for what can be inferred from the text of his book, which consists of five oracles about the Chaldeans (Babylonians) and a song of praise to God. Since the Chaldean rise to power is dated c. 612 BC, it is assumed he was active about that time, making him an early contemporary of Jeremiah and Zephaniah. Jewish sources, however, do not group him with those two prophets, who are often placed together, so it is possible that he was slightly earlier than they. Because the final chapter of his book is a song, it is sometimes assumed in Jewish tradition that he was a member of the tribe of Levi, which served as musicians in Solomon's Temple. According to the Zohar (Volume 1, page 8b) Habakkuk is the boy born to the Shunamite woman through Elisha's blessing. Habakkuk is unique among the prophets in that he openly questions the wisdom of God.[citation needed] In the first part of the first chapter, the Prophet sees the injustice among his people and asks why God does not take action: "1:2 Yahweh, how long will I cry, and you will not hear? I cry out to you 'Violence!' and will you not save?" - (World English Bible).
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 34: Nahum by American Standard Version
description:
Nahum prophesied, according to some, in the beginning of the reign of Ahaz (740s BC). Others, however, think that his prophecies are to be referred to the latter half of the reign of Hezekiah (700s BC). Probably the book was written in Jerusalem, where he witnessed the invasion of Sennacherib and the destruction of his host (2 Kings 19:35). And still others support the idea that the "book of vision" was written shortly before the fall of Nineveh (612 BCE). This theory is evidenced by the fact that the oracles must be dated after the Assyrian destruction of Thebes in 663 BCE as this event is mentioned in Nah 3:8.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 33: Micah by American Standard Version
description:
The book may be divided into three sections:
Chapters 1–3 mainly consist of oracles of judgment.
Chapters 4–5 of oracles of hope.
Chapters 6–7 begins with judgment and moves to hope.
Chapters 1–3 mainly consist of oracles of judgment. The judgment motif is so strong in this book that Micah only preached about judgment. Judgment in Micah is seen in the destruction of Samaria, in the coming of an invader against Jerusalem, in the greedy land-grabbers' loss of their land and in their being abandoned by Yahweh, in shame for the false prophets, in the siege of Jerusalem and the cleaning of the land from idolatry and militarism.
Chapters 4–5 consist of oracles of hope. The prophet said that those conditions would not prevail forever. Judgment would come but a saved, chastened, and faithful remnant would survive. A new king from the line of David would be replace the present weak king on the throne. He would reign in the majesty of the name of Yahweh. His people would dwell securely and he would be great to the ends of earths.
Chapters 6–7 begin with judgment and move to hope. Micah puts a protest on the people's lips, offering any religious response God cared to ask for. God's indictment becomes specific in 6:9–16. Violence, deception, and crooked business practices were rampant. They would bring desolation and destruction to the land. The reference to Omri and Ahab indicates that the same kinds of corruption that destroyed the northern kingdom had now spread to Judah.
In conclusion, Micah's later hearers take his messages to heart. His words of hope gave them new heart to live as God's people in a darkened world.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 32: Jonah by American Standard Version
description:
In the Hebrew Bible, the Book of Jonah is the fifth book in a series of books called the Minor Prophets. Unlike other prophetic books however, this book is not a record of a prophet’s words toward Israel. Instead of the poetry and prophetic prose of Isaiah or Lamentations, this book tells the story of a reluctant prophet who arguably becomes one of the most effective prophets in the entire Bible.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 31: Obadiah by American Standard Version
description: "The Book of Obadiah is found in both the Hebrew Bible and the Old Testament of the Christian Bible, where it is the shortest book, only one chapter long. Its authorship is generally attributed to a person named Obadiah, which means “servant (or worshipper) of the Lord”. Obadiah is classified as a "minor prophet" in the Christian Bible due to the brevity of the writing (only 21 verses) and the content (prophetic material). An Old Testament prophet was (professedly) not only a person who was given divine insight into future events, but a person whom the Lord used to declare his word. The first nine verses in the book foretell total destruction in the land of Edom at the hand of the Lord. Obadiah writes that this destruction will be so complete that it will be even worse than a thief who comes at night, for not even a thief would destroy everything. The Lord will allow all allies of Edom to turn away and help chase Edom out of its land. What is the reason for such a harsh punishment? Verses ten through fourteen explain that when Israel (the Lord’s chosen people) was attacked, Edom refused to help them, thus acting like an enemy. What is even worse is that Edom and Israel share a common blood line through their founders who were brothers, Jacob and Esau. Because of this gross neglect of a relative, Edom will be covered with shame and destroyed forever. The final verses, fifteen through twenty-one, depict the restoration of Israel and the wiping out of the Edomites. Verse eighteen says that there will be no survivors from the house of Esau once the destruction is complete. Israel will become a holy place and its people will return from exile and inhabit the land once inhabited by the Edomites. The final verse of the prophecy places the Lord as King who will rule over all the mountains of Edom." (Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 30: Amos by American Standard Version
description: "The Book of Amos is one of the books of the Nevi'im (Hebrew: "prophets") and of the Christian Old Testament. Amos is one of the minor prophets. Amos was the first biblical prophet whose words were recorded in a book, an older contemporary of Hosea and Isaiah. He was active c 750 BC during the reign of Jeroboam II. He lived and prophesied in the southern kingdom of Judah. His major themes of social justice, God's omnipotence, and divine judgment became staples of prophecy." (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 27: Daniel by American Standard Version
description:
The Book of Daniel (דניאל), originally written in Hebrew and Aramaic, is a book in both the Hebrew Bible (Tanakh) and the Christian Old Testament. The book is set during the Babylonian Captivity, a period when Jews were deported and exiled to Babylon following the Siege of Jerusalem of 597 BC. The book revolves around the figure of Daniel, an Israelite who becomes an adviser to Nebuchadnezzar, the ruler of Babylon from 605 BC - 562 BC. The book has two distinct parts: a series of six narratives (chapters one to six) and four apocalyptic visions (chapters seven to twelve). The narratives take the form of court stories which focus on tests of religious fidelity involving Daniel and his friends (chapters one, three and six), and Daniel's interpretation of royal dreams and visions (chapters two, four and five). In the second part of the book, Daniel recounts his reception of dreams, visions and angelic interpretations in the first person.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 29: Joel by American Standard Version
description: "The Book of Joel is part of the Jewish Tanakh, and also the Old Testament of the Christian Bible. Joel is part of a group of twelve prophetic books known as the Minor Prophets or simply as The Twelve; the distinction 'minor' indicates the short length of the text in relation to the larger prophetic texts by the "Major Prophets" known as the Nevi'im." (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 28: Hosea by American Standard Version
description: Hosea was the son of Beeri and a prophet in Israel in the 8th century BCE. He is one of the Twelve Prophets of the Jewish Hebrew Bible, also known as the Minor Prophets of the Christian Old Testament. We know practically nothing about the life or social status of Hosea. According to the Book of Hosea, he married the prostitute Gomer, the daughter of Diblatayim, at God's command. He lived in the Northern Kingdom in the period 740–725 BCE. In Hosea 5:8 ff., there is a reference to the wars which led to the capture of the kingdom by the Assyrians (ca. 734–732 BCE). It is not certain if he has also experienced the destruction of Samaria, which is foreseen in Hosea 14:1. Hosea's family life reflected the "adulterous" relationship which Israel had built with polytheistic gods. His children's names made them like walking prophecies of the fall of the ruling dynasty and the severed covenant with God — much like the prophet Isaiah a generation later. Hosea is often seen as a "prophet of doom", but underneath his message of destruction is a promise of restoration." (From Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 25: Lamentations by American Standard Version
description:
The Book of Lamentations (Hebrew: אֵיכָה‎, Eikha, ʾēḫā(h)) is a book of the Bible Old Testament and Jewish Tanakh. It is traditionally read by the Jewish people on Tisha B'Av, the fast day that commemorates the destruction of the Temple in Jerusalem. It is called in the Hebrew canon 'Eikhah, meaning "How," being the formula for the commencement of a song of wailing. It is the first word of the book (see 2 Sam. 1:19-27). The Septuagint adopted the name rendered "Lamentations" (Greek threnoi = Hebrew qinoth) now in common use, to denote the character of the book, in which the prophet mourns over the desolations brought on Jerusalem and the Holy Land by the Chaldeans. In the Hebrew Bible it is placed among the Ketuvim, the Writings.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 23: Isaiah by American Standard Version
description:
"The Book of Isaiah (Hebrew: ספר ישעיה‎) is a book of the Bible traditionally attributed to the Prophet Isaiah, who lived in the second half of the 8th century BC. In the first 39 chapters, Isaiah prophesies doom for a sinful Judah and for all the nations of the world that oppose God. The last 27 chapters prophesy the restoration of the nation of Israel. This section includes the Songs of the Suffering Servant, four separate passages that Christians believe prefigure the coming of Jesus Christ, and which are otherwise traditionally thought to refer to the nation of Israel. This second of the book's two major sections also includes prophecies of a new creation in God's glorious future kingdom."
(Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 22: Song of Solomon (version 2) by American Standard Version
description:
The Song of Songs (Hebrew title שיר השירים, Shir ha-Shirim), is a book of the Hebrew Bible—Tanakh or Old Testament—one of the five megillot (scrolls). It is also known as the Song of Solomon or as Canticles, the latter from the shortened and anglicized Vulgate title Canticum Canticorum, "Song of Songs" in Latin. It is known as Aisma in the Septuagint, which is short for Αισμα ᾀσμτων, Aisma aismatôn, "Song of Songs" in Greek.
(From Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 21: Ecclesiastes (version 2) by American Standard Version
description: "Ecclesiastes is a wisdom book of the Old Testament. The author represents himself as the son of David, and king over Israel in Jerusalem. The work consists of personal or autobiographic matter, at times expressed in aphorisms and maxims illuminated in terse paragraphs with reflections on the meaning of life and the best way of life. The work emphatically proclaims all the actions of man to be inherently "vain", "futile", "empty", or "meaningless," depending on translation, as the lives of both wise and foolish men end in death. While the teacher clearly promotes wisdom as a means for a well-lived earthly life, he is unable to ascribe eternal meaning to it. In light of this perceived senselessness, the preacher suggests that one should enjoy the simple pleasures of daily life, such as eating, drinking, and taking enjoyment in one's wife and work, which are gifts from the hand of God." (From Wikipedia, modified by Sam Stinson)

title: Bible (ASV) 20: Proverbs by American Standard Version
description:
Proverbs, a book of the Old Testament, is a collection of pithy Biblical sayings. It is wisdom literature by multiple authors, including Solomon. Throughout the book, Lady Wisdom and Lady Folly struggle to get the attention of the simple, leading them to glory or disgrace.
(Summary by Sam Stinson)

This is a space for raw journal reflections, intimate conversations, and soul-shaping truths that call us deeper into healing, restoration, and obedience to God.
Through open “Innerludes” from my own walk and guest dialogues rooted in transparency and faith, we’ll explore what it means to abide, obey, and live fully in Christ. No fluff. No surface talk. Just honest moments that press us closer to His heart.

title: Creation Life Flourish
author: Tiana
description:
Creation Life Flourish is a Christian space for spiritual encouragement, honest conversations, and practical tools to support your walk with God.
Through podcast episodes, digital journals, and intentional content, this channel supports spiritual and emotional growth and invites deeper reflection rooted in Biblical truth.
🎙️ Subscribe for thoughtful conversations on faith, identity, healing, and Christian living.

title: Anchored In Grace Marriage Podcast
author: Eric Nicholson
description: Our vision is to answer God’s call by using our marriage—its experiences, lessons, and redemption—to help couples seek God for true transformation. Through Christ, we stand to reclaim marriages from the enemy, reminding the world that marriage belongs to God, was redeemed by Christ’s sacrifice, and is meant to reflect His love, grace, and covenant faithfulness. We envision marriages elevated, restored, and anchored in God’s truth so they may glorify Him and strengthen families for generations to come.

title: House of Kpadeh

description: Just a married couple who loves each other and Yahweh, sharing whatever He tells us to.

description:
Welcome to the home of the original short scary stories podcast Formerly called Dark tales of the night that name waas a little too popular here you will find stories that we write to scare you and some times maybe make you think about the monster in all of us so come on in have a seat and enjoy the podcast

  If you would like to support this podcast you can go to our website every day and listen to one of our episodes even for just 60 seconds that would help us immensely .

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/dark-tales-from-the-edge--7153491/support.

description: Investment research and stock recommendations

title: Hand Me Sounds
author: iHeartPodcasts
description: Hand Me Sounds is a music history podcast that explores the depths of genre and creativity through thematic analysis of cover songs, interpolated lyrics, musical homage, sampling, influence, idea exchange, and more from the framing of a consignment store run by store manager, and host, Shi.City. Each episode spotlights a targeted topic, features thoughtful historical commentary and aims to expand the average music fan’s mind and appreciation for popular art. Musical history is uncovered by going through the racks and bins of cultures past to reveal the hidden, most often times Black and feminist histories behind some of the most enduring songs of all time. Music, like second-hand clothing, can stitch unexpected knots between generations, at Hand Me Sounds we’re her to show how music history and pop culture fit together seamlessly. Hand Me Sounds is a virtual music history shop exploring genre and creativity through analysis of cover songs, interpolated lyrics, sampling, & more.

description: audio de ingles

title: Stoic Mental Control
description:
Master your emotions and cultivate inner peace with Stoic Mental Control. Discover practical wisdom from ancient Stoic philosophy to navigate modern challenges and build unshakeable resilience in your daily life.

Stoic Mental Control offers daily insights and actionable strategies to help you achieve profound self-mastery. We delve into core Stoic principles like virtue, reason, and acceptance, translating timeless teachings into practical exercises for emotional regulation and improved decision-making. This podcast empowers you to develop a robust mental framework, fostering tranquility and purpose.

New episodes arrive every day, Monday through Sunday, at 8:00 AM. Each short, impactful session provides a focused reflection or a powerful technique to integrate Stoicism into your routine, making ancient wisdom accessible and relevant. Expect clear, concise guidance to strengthen your character and mental fortitude.

This podcast is for anyone seeking to overcome anxiety, manage stress, and live a more deliberate, fulfilling life through the power of Stoic philosophy. If you're ready to take charge of your inner world and find serenity amidst chaos, you've found your daily guide. Subscribe now to Stoic Mental Control and begin your journey to a more composed and purposeful existence.

title: Backable
author: Tim Krotiris
description:
If you could listen to every conversation within the walls of a High Performance Business Growth Consultancy, do you believe you could improve your business? At worst, could you at least reinforce your own opinions, optimise ideas and use some insights to accelerate your performance each day? This is the next best thing. 
Covering key business ideas from areas including leadership, partnerships and the realities of business challenges. What does it really take to grow? Access insights from the Philotimo Performance Centre, industry experts, quiet achievers and business builders sharing their experiences.

title: The AI Desk
author: The Apex Cipher
description: Welcome to The AI Desk, your zero code guide to making artificial intelligence actually work for you. If you are tired of confusing tech jargons and don't want to learn programming after a long day at your job, you are in the right place.We break down complex tech into simple stories. Learn how to set up on your own "digital crew" to handle boring repetitive tasks while you commute or walk the dog. No coding, no stress- just real shortcuts to win your free time back. You love it because it's AI built for real people who just want to get things done.

title: Kiddo Cleat Report

description: My 9 year old soccer buff likes to share his opinions, predictions & news updates with all the fellow soccer fans!! Tune in for some fun banter!

title: Pastures for the Planet
author: Horses for Clean Water
description: Brought to you by Horses for Clean Water, Pastures for the Planet is the podcast for equestrians who want to learn how to work with nature, not against it. We share simple, actionable steps for helping your horse property build climate resilience and become a haven for native wildlife.

title: pastor paulHills Kairos odey's podcast
author: Pastor PaulHills Kairos Odey
description: Gospel is the light

title: Make Your Net Work
author: Nikko Dumas
description:
Your gifts are your net. Your people are your wealth. Make Your Net Work is where faith, relationships, and purpose collide. Hosted by singer & author Nikko Dumas.
New episodes weekly. Come as you are. 🎙️

title: Social Bullshit Podcast with Relly and Binxx
author: terrellrodrickcompany
description: Social Bullshit is the raw, uncut podcast from Relly (Detroit native) and Brinxx (Texas native), romantic partners navigating life as an LGBTQ, polyamorous couple. Resurfacing from About It and Living It, they bring real talk, hard lessons, laughs, tears,

title: Mental GPS with Dr. Jana
author: Dr. Jana Price-Sharps
description:
Mental GPS with Dr. Jana - Navigating the Mind

Welcome to Mental GPS with Dr. Jana a self-development podcast dedicated to helping you unstick your mind, regain momentum, and build a better life. Hosted by Dr. Jana Price-Sharps, this show is built on a central philosophy: life shouldn’t just be endured it should be enjoyed. You have an absolute right to be happy, live a good life, and experience fulfilling relationships. 


Think of your brain as having its own internal navigation system. Where is your GPS driving you?. If your coordinates are set to failure, depression, bitterness, or hopelessness, your mindset will drive you straight off a cliff. This podcast is designed to help you actively reprogram your navigation system, take control of your mental focus, and successfully steer your way through life's adversity.

title: PeopleAmongUs.
author: Ricardo Balzis
description:
Everyone has a story. Most are never told.
PeopleAmongUs. explores the person behind the profession through conversations about careers, identity, belonging, and the decisions that shape our lives.
Each episode features people building international careers, changing direction, overcoming setbacks, taking risks, and defining success on their own terms.
No polished answers. Just people among us.
📩 Feedback, questions or guest suggestions?
peopleamongus.pod@gmail.com

title: The Charing Cross Mystery: A High Stakes Murder
description: A high ranking police official is found dead, and the question lingers: who killed him? As the investigation unfolds, the discovery of a glamorous photo of one of two high-profile sisters in the victim's pocket raises suspicions. J. S. Fletcher masterfully weaves a web of intriguing characters, including a determined lawyer, his resourceful assistant, and a clever police spy. Each character follows their own leads, revealing secrets and motives that intertwine in unexpected ways. The stakes rise as the detectives race against time to uncover the truth and unravel the connections between the murder and the enigmatic sisters. With twists and turns at every corner, this mystery keeps listeners on the edge of their seats, eager to piece together the clues. Dive into a world of suspense and intrigue where nothing is as it seems, and every character holds a key to the enigma. Will justice prevail, or will the secrets of Charing Cross remain buried forever?

title: Wise Women's Conversations
author: Angela Counsel
description: Wise Women's Conversations, is a podcast for women who want to thrive in the second half of life.  Hosted by naturopath and health coach Angela Counsel, each episode explores health, wellbeing, mindset, purpose and the wisdom that comes from lived experience. Through practical insights and inspiring conversations, you'll discover new ways to age well, stay curious, embrace new adventures and create a future that feels fulfilling and meaningful.  Because your story is far from over.

title: Win By Two Volleyball Podcast
author: Jessy Satti
description:
The Official Podcast of Pakmen Volleyball. 
Hosted by Jessy Satti
Win by Two features conversations with athletes, coaches, and leaders from across the volleyball world. 
Inside the game, building champions, and behind the culture.

title: This is My Faith!
author: The Church of The Good Shepherd, Brighton
description: We interview members of our congregation to find out how they became a Christian, why their faith is important to them, and what keeps them coming back Sunday after Sunday.

title: The cellular revival podcast
author: Breeze Thies
description: Here you will hear things about deep cellular health and wellness that you will never hear from your doctor. I will talk about the things that helped me recover from years of chronic illness. I have learned from people all over the world who have revolutionized the healing space and gotten the unwell well again, including themselves. My focus is on foundational things that fix the cellular membranes, and those allow our bodies to heal and function from the ground up.  Come learn out of the box with me.

title: Aimed At Us: Podcast by youth for youth
author: Aimed At Us
description:
Aimed At Us is a powerful podcast that sheds light on the realities of gun violence and the strength of those affected by it. Each episode focuses on the stories from every side - from offenders seeking redemption to survivors, families, judges, lawyers, doctors, and advocates - to share their stories. Through open and courageous conversations, we uncover not only the pain and impact but also the resilience, healing, and hope for change. 
We are Aimed At Us a podcast made by youth for youth with New Mexico To Prevent Gun Violence.

title: The Ellipsis
author: Studio Ellipsis
description:
The Ellipsis is an unfiltered conversation between the three founders of Studio Ellipsis--the kind that usually starts after the meeting ends and the cameras are off.
No press lines. No polished panels. No corporate talking points.
Just three people who’ve made games at every level, talking honestly about the things the industry thinks constantly, says quietly, and almost never says in public: creative risk, business reality, where the culture is heading, and what it really takes to build something new.
An ellipsis is everything that goes unsaid.
This is us saying it.

title: Marc & Laura Need An Audience
author: Marc & Laura
description:
We're Marc & Laura and we need an audience. Too bad for us, we're from Steinbach, Manitoba...Where the appetite for new theatre is basically zero.
So, we're taking a big swing. Season 1 of this podcast is called: "Chicago". And you'll hear all about how we've decided to throw our caps over the wall and take on the Windy City, in the blind hope that theatre towns actually do still exist.

title: Stoic Life Guided
description:
Unlock ancient wisdom for modern challenges. Stoic Life Guided offers practical, daily insights to cultivate inner peace and resilience in a chaotic world.

This podcast is your essential companion for applying Stoic philosophy to everyday life. We explore timeless teachings from Marcus Aurelius, Seneca, and Epictetus, translating complex concepts into actionable strategies for self-mastery, emotional regulation, and ethical living. Expect clear, concise guidance designed to help you navigate stress, make better decisions, and find tranquility.

New episodes arrive daily, Monday through Sunday, bright and early at 8:00 AM, ensuring you start each day with a dose of profound wisdom. Each session provides a focused reflection or a practical exercise, perfect for integrating Stoicism into your morning routine and carrying its benefits throughout your day.

Stoic Life Guided is for anyone seeking to develop mental fortitude, improve their well-being, and live a more purposeful life guided by reason and virtue. If you're looking for a consistent source of inspiration and practical philosophy, this is your daily anchor.

Subscribe now and transform your perspective, one guided reflection at a time.

title: Cock and Cannon
author: Cock and Cannon
description:
The Cock and Cannon is a fortnightly football and Sorare podcast hosted by two rival North London fans — Jeddy (Spurs, @Jeddyknight6) and Johno (Arsenal, @PranksyBreaks).
Each episode they break down the latest in Sorare, football, and physical football cards, mixing sharp analysis with relentless derby-day banter — pack openings, wonderkid tips, transfer window chat, and two hosts convinced they're always right (only one of them actually is).
A rotating third chair joins every episode — a mate, a rival, or a total wildcard.
New episodes every two weeks. Subscribe now and don't miss kickoff.

title: Origins of Ambition
author: Origins of Ambition
description: This podcast tells the true stories of extraordinary people who started with very little and built remarkable success. Each episode explores the setbacks, sacrifices, psychology, and defining moments behind greatness—offering not just inspiration, but honest insight into resilience, ambition, and what success really demands.

title: The Women's Basketball Podcast
author: Hannah Alty, Caroline Bullock, Sam Heitzman
description:
Amplifying the Women's Game
From the elite competition of the Women's Super League Basketball to the grassroots pioneers driving the sport forward. Hosted by Hanah Alty and Caroline Bullock, we cover the stories, game analysis, and figures shaping UK basketball.

title: The Wilder Nottinghamshire Podcast
author: emcdaid
description:
The Wilder Nottinghamshire Podcast has been created to counter the doom and gloom that is often a feature of environmental reporting. 
Each month, the podcast will feature the people and projects that are taking positive action for nature across Nottinghamshire -highlighting the huge range of different ways people are supporting nature recovery and the passion for wildlife that gives hope for the future. 
In this episode, you’ll discover more about efforts to monitor and expand the population of hazel dormice in Nottinghamshire - a species once deemed extinct in our county. Hear the excitement as our host, Rob Carlyle, sees his first dormouse and find out the difference between a ’pinkie’ and a ’chubster’! 
Our podcast team
In the spirit of the Wilder Nottinghamshire Network, the podcast is a team effort, brought to you by presenter Rob Carlyle, a long-term member of Nottinghamshire Wildlife Trust and sustainable gardening blogger; David Heathcote, our audio editor, with decades of broadcast experience in TV and Radio – with Erin McDaid, the Trust’s Communications, Campaigns & Advocacy Lead providing a guiding hand.

title: PeopleTalks: Recruit, Develop, Retain
author: We Are Zenith
description: PeopleTalks explores the strategies, stories, and insights that help organisations attract great people, develop talent, and build workplaces where employees thrive. Featuring conversations with HR leaders, recruiters, learning professionals, and business leaders shaping the future of work.

title: Somebody Call Cut!
author: Martha Prosper, Alhaji Sesay, Robert Smith
description: There are moments in life when things get so messy you wish someone would yell, “CUT.” Hosted by three actors on their grind, Somebody Call CUT! is an unscripted look at adulting, relationships, and career chaos -explored through laughter, honest conversations, and inspiring guests.

title: Reinvent With Balance – Hendrina Sterling Rodriguez
author: Hendrina Sterling Rodriguez - Produced by © 2026 HSR Legacy LLC
description:
Reinvent With Balance – with Hendrina Sterling Rodriguez is a podcast exploring mental health, self-image, relationships, body changes, and personal transformation, especially for women after 30. Hosted by award-winning bilingual psychotherapist and clinical hypnotherapist Hendrina Sterling Rodriguez, each episode offers honest reflections and practical psychological insights on confidence, identity, emotional wellbeing, hormones, anxiety, healing, and reconnecting with yourself. 
This is real inner transformation — not surface-level motivation.
Produced by © 2026 HSR Legacy LLC

title: Still Not Dead
author: Martin Eade
description:
One couples journey with Leukemia into a new life.
In 2026 Martin and Natalie had live events planned around the world, their respective businesses were flourishing, they were creating the life of their dreams together.
Until a routine blood test and a call from the Dr changed all that. "Martin you have Leukemia".
This podcast is authentic real talk from two high performance coaches who share the stories and techniques that help get them through every day and how they have pivoted their thinking and lives for the future.

title: The Two Wolves Podcast
author: Graeme & Carter
description:
Inside of you there are two wolves. One is a gangly armed egomaniac who can snaps forearms like celery stalks. The other has buns of steel and a propensity for overusing the same spinning back kick (and is also an egomaniac).

Join Graeme and Carter, two otherwise unremarkable white men who also happen to be the only people on Earth brave enough to judge the two most middle-of-the-road action stars of the late 1980s and 1990s: Jean Claude Van Damme and Steven Seagal.

Building acanonical ranking of combined filmographies that will stand for all time, our hosts will pit each actor's films against each other in head to head matchups until they've rated every single film–or until they can't stand watching them anymore!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Waffle Podcast
author: Lisa Burton
description:
A concrete slab might seem like a technical detail, but the pod system behind it can influence cost, labour, waste, embodied carbon and long-term environmental performance.

This podcast looks at why EPS Waffle Pods may offer a more efficient solution for builders and developers, including lower material use, reduced site waste, recycling through industry-led stewardship programs, and the benefits of a closed-loop approach to construction materials.

A practical conversation for developers, builders and site teams looking to make more informed decisions from the ground up.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Breach One Teach One
author: Dominic Alegrete
description:
Breach One Teach One is a podcast/webinar series for people who want to land a career in cybersecurity, become financially free, and maximize their health and wellness.
Hosted by Dominic Alegrete, a SOC Analyst who took an untraditional path into tech. He started in high school, landed a Fortune 500 company tech role by 18, and by 20 had his first cybersecurity role without a college degree.
Every episode features world-renowned professionals sharing what it takes to build a career, become financially free, and take full control of your health.

title: Original Tangents
author: O.G.T.
description: A show that covers anything and everything… and nothing at all.

title: Can We Move This Up?
author: Katie McAndrews
description:
The biggest breakthroughs often happen when the plan changes, and it’s time we talk about it. This show explores the human side of creative careers - the pivots, the pressure, and personal growth we all face.
In each episode, we connect with an industry expert, a hiring leader, or an entrepreneur to learn about how they got to where they are today! 
This podcast gets into the real story behind creative journeys, mental health, burnout, and how to stay resilient. Let's go!

title: The Cosmic Pattern Room
author: Alla Tormey
description:
The Cosmic Pattern Room is a podcast for people who feel everything, notice too much, and are ready to understand the patterns shaping their life.
Hosted by Alla Tormey, astrologer, tarot reader, filmmaker, and professional dot-connector, this show explores astrology, intuition, timing, relationships, identity, self-worth, creativity, and the strange crossroads where life asks you to choose more consciously.
Expect sharp cosmic weather reports, psychological insights, intuitive pattern recognition, relationship reality checks, and grounded spiritual commentary with a slightly oddball pulse.

title: SSCPA TAX EDUCATE

description:
Understanding taxes shouldn't feel overwhelming. That's why SSCPA Tax Educate breaks down tax laws, policies, and the latest updates into simple, practical guidance you can actually use. From deductions to IRS changes to breaking tax news - we help you stay informed, confident, and prepared.
Follow us for clear answers, real insights, and smarter tax decisions any day.

title: We the People 250
author: Dennis Wilson
description:
A discussion and analysis of the United States Constitution from a historical, political and modern perspective. 
My goal is to explore the forms of government as political philosophers discuss as we put together basic principals of republicanism. As we move forward, we will discuss the colonial period and the colonists discussions on democracy. We will discuss the revolutionary war and how we formed our local, state and national governments.

author: 3rd Paradigm Podcast
description: Join your hosts, Clarity and Nuance, in this "digital 3rd space" that fosters open, thought-provoking discussions, offering guests the option to use an alias identity while sharing meaningful perspectives on contemporary issues and topics. As part of an evolving conversation, guests are encouraged to take the lead as hosts for their very own episodes, guided by the 3rd Paradigm Podcast team to ensure an impactful and respectful storytelling experience.

title: Worth Beyond Earth
author: Marina
description:
🌍✨ Worth Beyond Earth is a space for souls who've ever felt like they're from somewhere else. Each episode is part of a larger journey — through spirituality, philosophy, crystals, and starseed origins — moving across dimensions and lifetimes in search of one truth: what is your worth, here on Earth and beyond it? 
🔮💎 Subscribe and join a community of seekers, starseeds, and free thinkers remembering who they are, where they came from, and why they're here.

title: The Borough Treasurer: Secrets in Highmarket
description: In the bustling town of Highmarket, Messrs. Mallalieu and Cotherstone have built their lives on success and respect. As the Mayor and Treasurer, they seem untouchable—until an unexpected arrival shakes the foundations of their carefully crafted reputations. An ex-detective with a murky past moves to town, bringing with him long-buried secrets that threaten to unravel everything they’ve worked for. As whispers of old crimes resurface, the community finds itself drawn into a web of intrigue, where trust is scarce and danger lurks at every corner. Can the two men protect their hard-earned status, or will the truth expose them in a way they never imagined? Join this thrilling journey through deception and mystery that keeps you guessing until the final reveal. Experience the tension and excitement as the characters confront their past and fight for their future in this captivating crime fiction tale.

title: 21st TSC Sustainers News Brief
author: 21st Theater Sustainment Command
description: The 21st TSC Sustainers News Brief is an audio brief summarizing news of note for 21st Theater Sustainment Command. This brief is informational only and does not imply endorsement of any organization. Generative artificial intelligence was used in the production of this brief, and copyright owners retain all rights to their work.

title: Me & My Podcast
author: Rosa Larios
description: This podcast is dedicated to my Philosophy on life, the things I have experience so far and my ever-changing views on various different topics. My tips on life :*

title: The Clue of the Twisted Candle: London Detective Mystery
description: In the heart of London, a mysterious murder unfolds, shrouded in intrigue and deception. 'Clue of the Twisted Candle' invites listeners into a world where every shadow hides a secret. As our determined detective delves deeper into the case, a twisted romance complicates the investigation, revealing unexpected connections and motivations. The plot thickens with each twist, leading to a shocking revelation that will keep you on the edge of your seat. Edgar Wallace masterfully weaves together suspense and romance, creating a captivating narrative that explores human nature's darker sides. This audiobook promises not just a thrilling mystery, but an exploration of trust, betrayal, and the lengths one will go to uncover the truth. Join the chase in this classic detective tale, where every clue matters and nothing is as it seems.

title: Don't Forget The Keys
author: BRG Media
description: Welcome to Don't Forget The Keys, hosted by Magan Battle. Whether you are buying your first home, growing a real estate business, or designing a life by design, this podcast delivers the essential strategies you need. Join us each week as we interview industry experts, share raw business insights, and unlock the wisdom required to build wealth and a legacy that lasts. Don't leave your success to chance—and don't forget the keys.

title: 2 Left Socks - The Podcast
author: 2 left Socks
description:
Two idiots. One microphone. Endless conversations.
Welcome to The 2 Left Socks Podcast.
Join Aden and Micci as they tackle everything from unsolved mysteries, conspiracy theories and paranormal encounters to weird news, internet rabbit holes, life’s random moments, and whatever else catches their attention that week.
We’re not experts. We’re not investigators. We’re just two mates with strong opinions, questionable theories, and a habit of getting completely sidetracked.
Expect plenty of laughs, genuine reactions, friendly arguments, ridiculous tangents, and the occasional deep dive into stories that leave us asking, “How is this even real?”
Whether we’re discussing a decades-old mystery, a bizarre conspiracy theory, or something completely random from the internet, one thing is guaranteed: you’ll never quite know where the conversation is heading.
Family by Choice. Idiots by Nature.
Welcome to The 2 Left Socks Podcast. 🧦

author: Joseph Okunmuyide

With your host: Etta Umbra

title: Online Business Around the World
author: Thee David
description:
Digital Business Tools — eSIM, VPN, Borderless Banking with Thee David


Welcome to Online Business Around the World, hosted by Thee David — a guide for entrepreneurs who refuse to let geography limit their business. Drawing on over 12 years of living and working across five countries, Thee David unpacks the real, practical side of staying connected, getting paid, and keeping your platforms accessible while operating internationally. Each episode blends personal experience with actionable strategy, giving online business owners the tools to build and run their business from anywhere in the world. This is the playbook nobody handed you — until now.

title: Talk To Me with Charlie McCarron
author: Charlie McCarron
description:
Talk To Me with Charlie McCarron is a podcast built around real conversations with people who have lived through something worth sharing.
Charlie sits down with business owners, leaders, athletes, parents, high performers and everyday people to talk about life, pressure, family, growth, success, setbacks and the lessons learned the hard way.
Thoughtful, honest and human, these are conversations worth slowing down for.

title: Hear Me Out…
author: Charlie Austin
description:
Welcome to Hear Me Out...
Every person has a story. Every profession has a side you've never seen. Every experience has the power to change the way we think.
Each week, Charlie sits down with people from all walks of life to explore their jobs, experiences, challenges and perspectives. From healthcare professionals and business owners to former prisoners, cancer survivors and lottery winners, every conversation offers a unique insight into another person's world.
No scripts. No sensationalism. Just honest conversations. You don't have to agree. Just hear them out.

title: Make us Lead
author: Waqar Mehmood
description: Make Us Lead is a podcast dedicated to recognizing leaders who serve their communities with sincerity, purpose, and compassion. We highlight both small local leaders around the world and well-known leaders, sharing their stories, challenges, and the positive impact they are making. Our goal is to inspire others to lead with good character, service, and a commitment to helping people.

title: Go Live Together
author: Go Live Together
description:
Welcome to “Go Live Together,” the ultimate morning show for cruising enthusiasts! Join your hosts as they kickstart your day with all things cruising, travel industry updates, and more!
Set sail with us as we dive into the latest cruising news, destination highlights, and insider tips to make your next voyage unforgettable.
Stay in the loop as we keep you updated on the cruise and travel industry, sharing the latest trends and developments that will shape your cruising experiences.

title: All About Love:
author: Billy Wu
description: Welcome to All About Love: a podcast inspired by the work of bell hooks, in which we try to understand the world through the lens of love and learn about love as a praxis.

title: Skills for Higher Education Pofessionals
author: Dr. Ibrahim Elsayed
description: Skills for Higher Education Professionals is the go-to podcast for higher ed staff, faculty, and administrators who want to elevate their careers and drive meaningful change on campus. Join us for bite-sized strategies, expert interviews, and real-world advice on leadership, student affairs, campus operations, and personal professional growth. Tune in to turn your daily campus challenges into stepping stones for success.

title: nili 's Podcast
author: nili
description:
همه ما زمان کافی برای شرکت در کلاس‌های آموزشی نداریم، اما همیشه می‌توانیم از زمان‌های آزادمان برای یادگیری استفاده کنیم.

این کانال با همین هدف ایجاد شده است؛ مجموعه‌ای از دوره‌های آموزشی متنوع که در قالب پادکست ارائه می‌شوند تا بتوانید هنگام رانندگی، پیاده‌روی، ورزش یا حتی استراحت، مهارت‌های جدید یاد بگیرید و دانش خود را افزایش دهید.

اینجا یادگیری ساده، در دسترس و بدون محدودیت است. کافی است پخش را بزنید و هر روز با یک موضوع جدید همراه شوید.

اگر به رشد فردی، توسعه مهارت‌ها و یادگیری مداوم اهمیت می‌دهید، این کانال می‌تواند همراه ارزشمند شما در این مسیر باشد.

title: VoyageMais: Boarding Pass
author: VoyageMais
description: Conversations about travel and expat trends in your language.

title: Mr. Justice Raffles: The Final Heist of a Gentlemen Thief
description: A. J. Raffles is no ordinary thief; he’s a British gentleman with a flair for the dramatic and a deep sense of justice. In this gripping final adventure, Raffles takes on a ruthless East End loan shark, aiming to teach him a lesson that will resonate throughout London. As he navigates the shadows of the city, Raffles's moral compass guides him through a web of danger and intrigue. This tale blends excitement with ethical dilemmas, showcasing Raffles’s charm and cunning as he balances his life of crime with a desire to do what’s right. Encounter thrilling escapades and unexpected twists as the legendary thief confronts his past and faces his greatest challenge yet. This audiobook is a perfect choice for fans of action, adventure, and complex characters who question the line between right and wrong.

title: kimia's Podcast

در این کانال، بهترین دوره‌های آموزشی در حوزه‌های مختلف را در قالب پادکست گردآوری و منتشر می‌کنیم تا بتوانید بدون نیاز به صرف زمان زیاد، مطالب کاربردی و ارزشمند را یاد بگیرید.

اگر باور دارید که یادگیری، سرمایه‌ای است که هیچ‌وقت از ارزش آن کم نمی‌شود، این کانال را دنبال کنید و همراه ما، هر روز چیز تازه‌ای بیاموزید.

این کانال با هدف ارائه دوره‌های آموزشی متنوع و کاربردی ایجاد شده تا بتوانید بدون محدودیت زمان و مکان، مهارت‌های جدید یاد بگیرید و دانش خود را گسترش دهید.

title: A Thief in the Night: The Adventures of A.J. Raffles
description: In the shadows of late Victorian England, A.J. Raffles, a charming gentleman and master thief, navigates the fine line between crime and cleverness. "Thief in the Night" brings together a thrilling collection of short stories that showcase Raffles' audacious heists and his knack for outsmarting the law. Each tale reveals not just the thrill of the chase but also the complexities of his character, blending wit, charm, and a hint of danger. As Raffles breaks into opulent homes, listeners will find themselves drawn into a world of high society and hidden secrets. With every twist and turn, this audiobook captures the excitement of adventure and the art of the steal. Perfect for fans of classic literature and those who appreciate clever plots, E.W. Hornung’s vivid storytelling invites you to explore the mind of a thief who feels more like an anti-hero than a criminal. Discover what drives Raffles and the risks he takes for the thrill of the heist.

title: The Man Who Was Thursday: A Nightmare of Anarchy
description: In a world on the brink of chaos, a secret society of anarchists plots to disrupt society and plunge it into darkness. Enter Gabriel Syme, a detective who infiltrates their ranks, only to find himself tangled in a web of deception and absurdity. G. K. Chesterton’s 'The Man Who Was Thursday' blends fantasy and humor with thrilling suspense, showcasing a battle between order and chaos. As Syme navigates this surreal landscape, he discovers that not everything is as it seems, and the true nature of his fellow conspirators is both shocking and enlightening. This novel challenges readers to ponder the complexities of identity, belief, and the human condition, all while keeping them on the edge of their seats. Chesterton's masterful storytelling invites listeners into a bizarre and thought-provoking adventure that remains relevant today, making it a must-listen for fans of mystery and philosophical fiction alike.

This thrilling collection of stories resumes their escapades, picking up after the dramatic fallout in 'The Gift of the Emperor.' Bunny, having served his time, finds himself restless and yearning for the excitement that only Raffles can provide. Together, they navigate the high stakes of crime, friendship, and moral ambiguity in the vibrant streets of London. Each story offers a fresh challenge and a glimpse into their complex relationship, filled with wit and cunning. Expect clever heists, nail-biting suspense, and a deeper understanding of the fine line that separates right from wrong. Perfect for fans of action and adventure, this audiobook invites you to experience Raffles' world where charm and danger walk hand in hand.

title: This Love In Action
author: Kevin & Raychel
description:
When you look at social media, you’re bombarded with either the filtered “perfect couple” façade or the chaotic drama of toxic love. And when it comes to friends and family? The behind-closed-doors truth often stays hidden.

On This Love In Action, we’re breaking that silence. Join us - a real, honest, growth-focused married couple - as we dive deep into the raw, unfiltered conversations everyone craves but rarely hears. 

From navigating attachment styles to healing family dynamics, tackling the tough stuff like trauma, sex, and everything in between, we’re pulling back the curtain on what it takes to create a thriving relationship.

It’s time to talk about love the way it really is - messy, transformative, and worth it.

After the Graft is where the working day ends and the questions begin.

Hosted by Paul, this is a podcast for people who are curious about the world and aren't afraid to ask difficult questions. From history, science and psychology to conspiracy theories, unexplained mysteries and conversations with interesting people, every episode explores ideas worth thinking about.

I'm not here to tell you what to believe. I'm not an expert, and I don't claim to have all the answers. My goal is simply to ask questions, explore different perspectives and have honest conversations that challenge assumptions and spark curiosity.

If you've ever found yourself wondering, "What if?", "Why?", or "Is there more to the story?"—you're in the right place.

Welcome to After the Graft... where the working day ends and the questions begin.

title: The Crystal Stopper: Arsène Lupin Mystery
description: A burglary at Deputy Daubrecq's home spirals into a tangled web of crime and injustice. When two accomplices of the notorious Arsène Lupin are arrested, one is guilty while the other is innocent. Both face the death penalty. Determined to save the wrongfully accused, Lupin finds himself pitted against Daubrecq, who wields a powerful secret hidden within a crystal stopper. As Lupin navigates deception and danger, he must outsmart a merciless blackmailer to uncover the truth. This gripping detective fiction combines clever twists with a moral dilemma, showcasing Lupin's wit and resourcefulness. Experience the thrill of justice in a world where appearances can be deceiving and every choice carries weight. Join Lupin on this thrilling quest to right a wrong and reveal the true nature of loyalty and betrayal.

title: The Club of Queer Trades: Quirky Detective Stories
description: In 'Mysteries of the Club of Queer Trades,' G. K. Chesterton invites you into a world where every detective story uncovers an eccentric profession and a delightful mystery. The stories revolve around Basil Grant, a former judge with a penchant for the peculiar. As he navigates through these tales, you’ll meet an array of colorful characters, each representing a unique trade that challenges the ordinary. From the whimsical to the outrageous, these narratives explore themes of identity, creativity, and the unexpected connections between people. Each story is packed with clever twists and hints of humor, making them perfect for anyone who loves a good mystery. Chesterton’s sharp wit and keen observations shine through, offering listeners not just a narrative but an experience that questions societal norms. Whether you’re a fan of classic literature or just in the mood for something refreshingly different, this audiobook promises to entertain and intrigue. Join Basil Grant on his adventures and discover the fascinating world of the Club of Queer Trades.

title: The Shadow Over Innsmouth - Unabridged
description: The Shadow Over Innsmouth by H. P. Lovecraft is a landmark of cosmic horror that immerses listeners in a chilling tale of mystery, isolation, and ancient terror. While traveling through the New England countryside, a young man makes an unexpected stop in the decaying seaport of Innsmouth. The town's deserted streets, strange inhabitants, and unsettling atmosphere immediately raise suspicion, but the true horror lies in the secrets whispered by the few willing to speak. As he uncovers the town's disturbing history, he is drawn into a nightmare where the boundaries between humanity and the unknown begin to blur. Filled with suspense, haunting imagery, and Lovecraft's signature sense of cosmic dread, this unforgettable story explores fear, identity, and the terrifying consequences of forbidden knowledge. A classic of supernatural fiction, it remains one of the most influential and captivating horror stories ever written. Summary by Dream Audiobooks

title: The Wisdom of Father Brown: Insights into Human Evil
description: In 'Wisdom of Father Brown,' G.K. Chesterton presents a collection of captivating short stories featuring his beloved detective, Father Brown. This unassuming Catholic priest, with his modest attire and large umbrella, possesses an extraordinary ability to see through the masks of human behavior. Unlike the traditional sleuths of his time, Father Brown relies on his deep understanding of human nature to unravel complex mysteries. Each story invites listeners into a world where morality and motives intertwine, challenging the notion of good and evil. As he navigates through dark and twisted plots, Father Brown’s intuitive insights reveal the hidden truths behind each crime. Discover how his faith and intellect illuminate the darkest corners of the human soul, offering thought-provoking reflections on justice and redemption. Perfect for fans of detective fiction, this audiobook promises to engage listeners with its rich storytelling and intricate plots, making it an essential addition to any mystery lover's collection.

title: Bible (ASV) 18: Job (version 2) by American Standard Version
description: God permits "Satan," or in some translations "the adversary" or "the accuser," to put the virtue of Job to the test, at first by giving him power over his property, but forbidding him to touch his person. Satan begins by taking away all of Job's riches, his livestock, his house, his servants, and his children; a series of four messengers informs him that they have perished in various tragedies. In the meantime, only three of Job's friends come to visit him in his misfortune — Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite. A fourth, Elihu the Buzite, first begins talking in chapter 32 and bears a distinguished part in the dialogue; his arrival is not noted. The friends spend a week sitting on the ground with Job, without speaking, until Job at last breaks his silence and complains of his misery. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 17: Esther by American Standard Version
description:
The book commences with a feast organized by Ahasuerus, initially for his court and dignitaries and afterwards for all inhabitants of Shushan. Ahasuerus orders his wife Vashti to display her beauty before the guests. She refuses, and the King's advisors warn that, if unpunished, her actions would inspire other wives to disobey their husbands. Ahasuerus removes her as queen. (Jews believe that she was executed, He then orders all young women to be presented to him, so he can choose a new queen to replace Vashti. One of these is Esther, who was orphaned at a young age and is being fostered by her uncle Mordechai."
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 15: Ezra by American Standard Version
description: The history of the first return of exiles, in the first year of Cyrus the Great (536 B.C.), till the completion and dedication of the new Temple in Jerusalem, in the sixth year of Darius (515 B.C.). The history of the second return under Ezra, in the seventh year of Artaxerxes Longimanus, and of the events that took place at Jerusalem after Ezra's arrival there. The book thus contains memorabilia connected with the Jews, from the decree of Cyrus to the reformation by Ezra (456 B.C.), extending over a period of about eighty years. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 13: 1 Chronicles by American Standard Version
description: The Books of Chronicles (Hebrew Divrei Hayyamim, דברי הימים, Greek Paralipomenon, Παραλειπομένων) are part of the Hebrew Bible (Jewish Tanakh and Christian Old Testament). In the Masoretic Text, it appears as the first or last book of the Ketuvim (the latter arrangement also making it the final book of the Jewish bible). Chronicles largely parallels the Davidic narratives in the Books of Samuel and the Books of Kings. It appears in two parts (I & II Chronicles), immediately following 1 & 2 Samuel and 1 & 2 Kings as a summary of them with minor details sometimes added. The division of Chronicles and its place in the Christian canon of the Old Testament are based upon the Septuagint. The author of Chronicles, termed "the Chronicler," may also have written Ezra and Nehemiah. His work is an important source of information about Israel after the Babylonian exile. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Tricera Talk
author: Avery North
description: Tricera Talk is a podcast where we explore the fascinating world of dinosaurs. Each episode features a college student as a guest, teaching us about a specific cretaceous topic, from the smallest details of dinosaur anatomy to debunking some epic prehistoric myths. Whether you’re a dinosaur enthusiast or just curious about the prehistoric world, Tricera Talk brings science and storytelling together into one roaring experience.

title: Bible (ASV) 10: 2 Samuel by American Standard Version
description: “The Books of Samuel (Hebrew: Sefer Sh’muel ספר שמואל) are part of the Tanakh (part of Judaism’s Hebrew Bible) and also of the Christian Old Testament. The work was originally written in Hebrew, and the Book(s) of Samuel originally formed a single text, as they are often considered today in Hebrew bibles. Together with what is now referred to as the Book(s) of Kings, the translators who created the Greek Septuagint divided the text into four books, which they named the Books of the Kingdoms. In the Latin Vulgate version, these then became the Books of the Kings, thus 1 and 2 Samuel were referred to as 1 and 2 Kings, with 3 and 4 Kings being what are called 1 and 2 Kings by the King James Bible and its successors.” (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 09: 1 Samuel by American Standard Version
description: The Books of Samuel (Hebrew: Sefer Sh'muel ספר שמואל) are part of the Tanakh (part of Judaism's Hebrew Bible) and also of the Christian Old Testament. The work was originally written in Hebrew, and the Book(s) of Samuel originally formed a single text, as they are often considered today in Hebrew bibles. Together with what is now referred to as the Book(s) of Kings, the translators who created the Greek Septuagint divided the text into four books, which they named the Books of the Kingdoms. In the Latin Vulgate version, these then became the Books of the Kings, thus 1 and 2 Samuel were referred to as 1 and 2 Kings, with 3 and 4 Kings being what are called 1 and 2 Kings by the King James Bible and its successors. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 08: Ruth by American Standard Version
description:
During the time of the Judges when there was a famine, an Israelite family from Bethlehem - Elimelech, his wife Naomi, and their sons Mahlon and Chilion - emigrate to the nearby country of Moab. Elimelech dies, and the sons marry two Moabite women: Mahlon marries Ruth and Chilion marries Orpah. Then Mahlon and Chilion also die. Naomi decides to return to Bethlehem. She tells her daughters-in-law to return to their own mothers, and remarry. Orpah reluctantly leaves; however, Ruth says, "Don't urge me to leave you or to turn back from you. Where you go I will go, and where you stay I will stay. Your people will be my people and your God my God. Where you die I will die, and there I will be buried. May the LORD deal with me, be it ever so severely, if anything but death separates you and me." (Ruth 1:16-17 NIV) The two women return to Bethlehem. It is the time of the barley harvest, and in order to support her mother-in-law and herself, Ruth goes to the fields to glean. The field she goes to belongs to a man named Boaz, who is kind to her because he has heard of her loyalty to her mother-in-law. Ruth tells her mother-in-law of Boaz's kindness, and she gleans in his field through the remainder of the harvest season. Boaz is a close relative of Naomi's husband's family. He is therefore obliged by the levirate law to marry Mahlon's widow, Ruth, in order to carry on his family line. Naomi sends Ruth to the threshing floor at night and tells her to "uncover the feet" of the sleeping Boaz. Ruth does so, Boaz awakes, and Ruth reminds him that he is "the one with the right to redeem." Boaz is willing to "redeem" Ruth, but there is a closer relative with a stronger right to do so. The next morning, Boaz discusses the issue with this man before the town elders. The other relative is unwilling to jeopardise the inheritance of his own estate by marrying Ruth, and so Boaz is free to do so. Boaz and Ruth get married and have a son named Obed (who by levirate customs is also considered a son or heir to Mahlon, and thus Naomi's grandson). In the genealogy which concludes the story, it is pointed out that Obed is the descendant of Perez the son of Judah, and the grandfather of David.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 07: Judges by American Standard Version
description: "The Book of Judges (Hebrew: Sefer Shoftim) is a book of the Bible originally written in Hebrew. It appears in the Tanakh and in the Christian Old Testament. Its title refers to its contents; it contains the history of Biblical judges (not to be confused with modern judges), who helped rule and guide the ancient Israelites, and of their times. As Judges stands today, the last judge it mentions is Samson, and although there are two further stories, the traditional view is that Samson's exploits probably synchronise with the period immediately preceding Eli, who was both high priest and judge. Both academic views and traditional thought hence view the narrative of the judges as ending at Samson, picking up again at 1 Samuel 1:1 to consider Eli, and continuing through to 1 Samuel 7:2. As for the stories at the end of the Book, which are set in the same time period as the judges but discuss people other than the judges, there is much affinity between these and the Book of Ruth, and many people believe Ruth originally belonged amongst them. There were thirteen Biblical Judges." (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 05: Deuteronomy by American Standard Version
description:
"Deuteronomy (Greek: Δευτερονόμιον, "second law") or Devarim (Hebrew: דְּבָרִים‎, literally "things" or "words") is the fifth book of the Hebrew Bible, and the fifth of five books of the Jewish Torah/Pentateuch. A large part of the book consists of five sermons delivered by Moses reviewing the previous forty years of wandering in the wilderness, and the future entering into the Promised Land. Its central element is a detailed law-code by which the Israelites are to live within the Promised Land. Theologically the book constitutes the renewing of the covenant between YHWH, the Jewish God, and the 'Children of Israel.'"
(Summary by Wikipedia)

It recounts the world from creation to the descent of the children of Israel into Egypt, and contains some of the best-known accounts of the Old Testament, including Adam and Eve, Cain and Abel, Noah's Ark, the Tower of Babel, and the biblical Patriarchs." title: Bible (ASV) 03: Leviticus by American Standard Version
description: The third book of the Torah (the Pentateuch) and Old Testament. In a series of speeches to Moses, God tells the Israelites and their priests, how to make offerings in the Tabernacle and how to conduct themselves while camped around the holy tent sanctuary. Leviticus takes place during the month or month-and-a-half between the completion of the Tabernacle (Exodus 40:17) and the Israelites' departure from Sinai (Numbers 1:1, 10:11). - Summary by Jessie Percival

title: Bible (ASV) 02: Exodus by American Standard Version
description:
"Moses leads the Hebrews out of Egypt and through the wilderness to the Mountain of God: Mount Sinai. There Yahweh, through Moses, gives the Hebrews their laws and enters into a covenant with them, by which he will give them the land of Canaan in return for their faithfulness. The book ends with the construction of the Tabernacle."
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ABU) NT 01-27: The New Testament by American Bible Union
description:
This Revised Testament has been prepared under the auspices of the American Bible Union, by the most competent scholars of the day. No expense has been spared to obtain the oldest translations of the Bible, copies of the ancient manuscripts, and other facilities to make the revision as perfect as possible.

The paragraph form has been adopted in preference to the division by verse, which is a modern mode of division, never used in the ancient scriptures. But, for convenience of reference, the numbers of the verses are retained.

All quotations from the Old Testament are distinctly indicated, and the poetic form is restored to those which appear as poetry in the original.

The revisers have been guided in their labors by the following rules prescribed by the Union:

RULES FOR THE REVISION OF THE ENGLISH NEW TESTAMENT.

The received Greek text, critically edited, with known errors corrected, must be followed.

The common English version must be the basis of revision, and only such alterations must be made as the exact meaning of the text and the existing state of the language may require.

The exact meaning of the inspired text, as that text expressed it to those who understood the original Scriptures at the time they were first written, must be given in corresponding words and phrases, so far as they can be found in the English language, with the least possible obscurity or indefiniteness.
(Summary by the American Bible Union Final Committee)

title: Scary True Dogman Stories
author: Sezgin
description:
Deep in the woods, something is watching. It walks on two legs. It has the head of a wolf. And the people who saw it will never be the same. Welcome to Scary True Dogman Stories — a chilling collection of real, firsthand encounters with the creature known as the Dogman.





These aren't just campfire tales. These are true Dogman sightings shared by hunters, hikers, truckers, and everyday people who came face to face with something that shouldn't exist. From dark forests to lonely back roads, each story will make you question what's really out there in the dark.





In every episode you'll find:





Real, terrifying Dogman encounters





Bone-chilling cryptid stories told in atmospheric detail





Perfect horror listening for late nights, long drives & the brave-at-heart





If you love true scary stories, cryptid encounters, Bigfoot and Dogman sightings, paranormal podcasts, and horror stories that keep you up at night, this is the show for you.





Turn off the lights, put on your headphones, and listen… if you dare.





Subscribe now so you never miss a new Dogman story — new terrifying true encounters every week.





#Dogman #TrueScaryStories #Cryptid #HorrorPodcast #Paranormal #ScaryStories #DogmanEncounters #Werewolf #Bigfoot #TrueHorror

title: Old Time Radio
description:
Old Time Radio brings back classic old time radio shows from the golden age of radio, featuring vintage radio dramas, mysteries, detective stories, westerns, comedy, science fiction, suspense, adventure, old Hollywood voices, and historic broadcasts from the era before television.

Each episode features carefully selected classic radio episodes for listeners who love old time radio, vintage storytelling, nostalgic entertainment, classic radio theater, retro audio drama, golden age radio shows, and timeless audio performances. Whether you are discovering old time radio shows for the first time or returning to favorite broadcasts from the past, this podcast is an easy place to find mystery radio programs, detective radio dramas, western radio adventures, old comedy shows, science fiction radio stories, suspense thrillers, and unforgettable broadcasts from another era.

Step back into the world of radio before television, when voices, music, sound effects, and imagination created entire worlds for millions of listeners. Old Time Radio preserves and shares the shows that defined generations of entertainment, bringing the atmosphere, charm, and storytelling power of vintage radio back to life.

title: Bib Ballads by Ring Lardner (1885 - 1933)
description: Ring Lardner is a typical parent when his first child is born, full of wonder and the rest of the usual emotions as he watches his little son grow. He wrote a series of 29 short poems on various facets of parenthood. (Summary by Jack Thro)

title: Bhagavad Gita by Unknown
description: The content of the text is a conversation between Krishna and Arjuna taking place on the battlefield of Kurukshetra just prior to the start of a climactic war. Responding to Arjuna's confusion and moral dilemma, Krishna explains to Arjuna his duties as a warrior and Prince and elaborates on a number of different Yogic and Vedantic philosophies, with examples and analogies. This has led to the Gita often being described as a concise guide to Hindu philosophy and also as a practical, self-contained guide to life. During the discourse, Krishna reveals his identity as the Supreme Being Himself (Bhagavan), blessing Arjuna with an awe-inspiring glimpse of His divine absolute form. (Wikipedia)

title: Initiated by GOD
author: Hardly Initiated Network
description:
