title: The Audacity
author: Zana Pali, Nadi Radhakrishna & Duda Nusevic
description:
THE AUDACITY is the new podcast set to shake up conversations around modern life, relationships, human behaviour, ambition and the things everyone thinks - but not everyone is willing to say out loud.


Hosted by property developer and former lawyer Zana Pali, lawyer and policy expert Duda Nusevic, and psychiatrist and soon-to-be published author Nadi Radhakrishna, The Audacity brings together three women with completely different backgrounds, personalities and ways of seeing the world - creating a dynamic where nothing is rehearsed, nothing is filtered and absolutely nothing is off limits.


Expect sharp opinions, uncomfortable truths, hilarious stories, relationship debates, dating disasters, social dynamics, friendship drama and those moments where everyone listening thinks: “I cannot believe someone actually said that.”


At its core, The Audacity explores the strange, funny and sometimes unbelievable realities of modern life - because whether it’s relationships, friendships, family dynamics, dating, social media, ambition or human behaviour, one thing remains constant:

People have audacity. And this podcast plans on talking about it.

title: Calling Forth the Creative Muse: A Story-Based Sleep Meditation Podcast
author: Nancy Fulton
description:
Welcome to Calling Forth the Creative Muse, a guided imagery and self-hypnosis podcast created for writers, artists, filmmakers, designers, performers, entrepreneurs, and all creative people who are ready to rest, dream, and create with greater ease.

Each episode is designed to help you quiet your busy creative mind, release the pressure to produce, and reconnect with the deep well of imagination within you. Through soothing guided imagery, sleep meditation, self-hypnosis, and poetic storytelling, you’ll be invited to relax your body, calm your thoughts, and drift into a state where inspiration can rise naturally.

Whether you are seeking help falling asleep, overcoming creative blocks, finding fresh ideas, or waking up feeling refreshed, renewed, and ready to bring something meaningful into the world, this podcast was created for you.

So settle in, close your eyes, and let yourself rest. Your creative mind does not need to struggle tonight. It only needs to listen, soften, dream, and remember its role in reimagining the world.

title: If We Can Keep It with Aaron Evans
author: Aaron Evans
description:
In 1787, Ben Franklin walked out of the Constitutional Convention on the last day. A woman named Elizabeth Powel stopped him and asked what they'd built in there. He said, "A republic, if you can keep it." This commemorative podcast celebrates some of the most perilous steps along the 250 year journey in the creation of a new nation.


I'm Aaron Evans. I run a PR firm and a strategic comms consultancy, and I've spent my career watching people try to build things that outlast them - companies, campaigns, coalitions, movements. Almost every story I care about in American history comes down to the same question Franklin was answering on the courthouse steps: is this republic worth the work it takes to hold it together, and are we the generation that's going to do the work?


Each episode is one story. Short. Focused. No panel, no politics-of-the-week, no shouting. I take one moment - Valley Forge in the snow, three guys in a room writing the Federalist Papers under a pen name, a Jewish broker on Front Street bankrolling Washington's army, the first peaceful handoff of power in 1800, Lincoln in 1858, Eisenhower's farewell, the Watergate tapes, the contested elections of 2000 and 2020 - and I pull it apart. What actually happened. Who did the hard work. What it cost them. And what it means for the people who are trying to build and lead something today.


This isn't a history lecture. It's a working conversation for the people I know are listening: business owners writing paychecks, executives running teams, officeholders trying to serve, and citizens who still believe the American experiment is worth defending.


New episodes weekly. If it hits, share it with someone who's building something that matters.


A republic. If we can keep it.

title: Beyond These Voices by Mary Elizabeth Braddon (1835 - 1915)
description: This novel tells the fictional life story of Veera Davis. The daughter of a poet and a poor noble woman, who is left in the care of her grandmother to a lonely and monotonous childhood, she has to make her way up in the world. But everything has a price. She tries to follow the conventions of the society which shunned her when she was poor. The novel explores themes such as love, money and loss. Don't worry, there is also a good murder mystery. Summary by Stav Nisser.

title: NEXT LEVEL YOU Podcast
author: mdabdulmalekkhan2023
description:
Welcome to NEXT LEVEL YOU Podcast – where growth begins, success is built, and your best life starts today.

At NEXT LEVEL YOU Podcast, we believe that every person has unlimited potential waiting to be unlocked. Our mission is to inspire, educate, and empower you with powerful insights that help you become the best version of yourself.

Whether you're looking for motivation, self-improvement, success strategies, personal development, mindset transformation, productivity tips, leadership skills, career growth, AI insights, or life-changing habits, you've come to the right place.

description: Some older episodes of Breakout W/ Steve that are no longer up for streaming I wanted to repost for everyone to enjoy!

title: Beyond the Vanishing Point by Ray Cummings (1907 - 1957)
description:
When George Randolph first caught sight of Orena, he was astounded by its gleaming perfection. Here were hills and valleys, lakes and streams, glowing with the light of the most precious of metals. And, more astonishing than that, it was a world of miniature perfection—an infinitely tiny universe within a golden atom!

But for Randolph it was also a world aglow with danger. Somewhere in its tiny vastness were the friends he had to rescue. Captives of a madman, they had been reduced to native Orena size; to return to Earth they needed the growth capsules Randolph was bringing them. It was up to Randolph to find them—and quickly—for the longer they stayed tiny, the closer they came to passing BEYOND THE VANISHING POINT! - Summary by Raymond King Cummings

title: Beyond the Stars by Charles Hanson Towne (1877 - 1949)
description: Charles Hanson Towne was a well known editor, author and poet. He was a popular New York celebrity who was considered by many as being the quintessential New Yorker. Besides being the editor at such magazines as Smart Set, Delineator, McClure’s, Designer, and Harper’s Bazaar, Towne also taught poetry at Columbia University. His most notable student was author  J.D. Salinger. At the age of 63, Town joined the company of the Broadway hit, Life with Father, and at 68 wrote his autobiography, So Far, So Good. - Summary by Nemo

title: Beyond the Rocks, A Love Story by Elinor Glyn (1864 - 1943)
description: Forced into marriage to a man who is older and wealthier, young Theodora Fitzgerald contemplates her fate in love, until she meets and falls in love with another. - Summary by Amy Deuchler

title: Enjoying God Always
author: Sound Surgery
description: Enjoying God Every Day is a faith-filled podcast inspired by Peter Jeffery’s powerful book Enjoy God Always.Each episode unpacks biblical truths, practical insights, and encouraging reflections to help you grow in faith, strengthen your spiritual life, and experience real peace in everyday living. Whether you're new to Christianity or looking to deepen your walk with God, Enjoying God Always will guide you toward a more joyful and Christ-centered life.

title: The Car Story Vault
author: The Car Story Vault
description:
Welcome to The Car Story Vault, your premium destination for cinematic audio documentaries dedicated to the legends of automotive history. We unlock the fascinating stories behind the world's most iconic luxury and performance machines, no matter the era.

Every episode is a meticulously crafted journey designed to put you right in the driver's seat. Experience the roar of engines pushing hundreds of kilometres per hour, the bitter rivalries that defined generations, and the brilliant visionaries who built enduring legends out of steel, oil, and ambition. Whether you are a lifelong gearhead, a history enthusiast, or simply a lover of a great story, the Vault is open. Join us on an immersive ride through the ultimate archives of automotive heritage.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Beyond the Horizon by Eugene O'Neill (1888 - 1953)
description:
Beyond the Horizon is a 1920 play written by American playwright Eugene O'Neill. It was O'Neill's first full-length work, and the winner of the 1920 Pulitzer Prize for Drama. The play focuses on the portrait of a family, and particularly two brothers Andrew and Robert. In the first act of the play, Robert is about to go off to sea with their uncle Dick, a sea captain while Andrew looks forward to marrying his sweetheart Ruth and working on the family farm as he starts a family. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Beyond the Breakers and other Poems by George Sterling (1869 - 1926)
description:
George Sterling was born on Long Island, New York but spent most of his life in California where he had a successful career in business and finance. In later years he settled in the small coastal community of Carmel-by-the-Sea.

Sterling's poetry was strongly influenced by the natural environment of Northern California, which was still largely unspoilt in his day, and particularly by the proximity of the vast Pacific Ocean.

(Summary by Alan Mapstone)

title: Unpacking Bipolar Disorder with Dr. Lori Kumar
author: Lori Kumar, DNP, PMHNP-BC, APRN
description:
Hello! My name is Dr. Lori Kumar. I am a doctoral-prepared, board-certified Psychiatric-Mental Health Nurse Practitioner with nearly a decade of specialized behavioral health experience. My clinical expertise spans complex mood and psychotic disorders, including schizophrenia and bipolar disorder, across a diverse array of settings—including community outpatient, acute inpatient, forensic, geriatric, substance-use, and interventional psychiatry.


I deliver evidence-based psychiatric evaluations, advanced psychopharmacology, rigorous risk assessments, and dedicated patient education for individuals from adolescence through older adulthood. Grounded in a trauma-informed and highly collaborative philosophy, my practice centers on empowering patients and families to fully understand their treatment options and achieve sustainable, long-term recovery.


Beyond the clinic, I believe I am a rising national educator and the creator of the platform and podcast, “Unpacking Schizophrenia and Bipolar Disorder with Dr. Lori Kumar,” which translates complex neuropsychiatric concepts into practical, clinically meaningful conversations. I presented to several hundred MDs, NPs and PAs at the annual Psych Congress Elevate, precepted the next generation of clinicians, and received patient-nominated recognition for my compassionate, patient-centered care.


I am dedicated to serving as a trusted voice in serious mental illness (SMI) education. I actively seek meaningful collaborations with healthcare organizations, pharmaceutical and medical affairs teams, clinicians, advocates, and industry partners committed to advancing the standard of psychiatric care.

title: Beyond the Black River by Robert E. Howard (1906 - 1936)
description: Conan the Barbarian is employed by one of the civilized countries to help in it's push to claim lands from the primitive Picts. The Picts are not excited about the idea however. Old gods and mythical creatures are called up by the Pict witches to contest the invading army and Conan finds himself battling for his life amid the blood thirsty hordes that include saber-toothed tigers, 40 foot long venomous snakes and a demon from another dimension who is intent on crushing him. The huge dog Slasher makes an appearance here and distinguishes himself so well in a doomed battle to delay their forces that Conan openly praises his courage and pledges that 7 Pict heads will roll in his honor. (Summary by Phil Chenevert)

title: Beyond Lies the Wub & The Skull by Philip K. Dick (1928 - 1982)
description: Two stories in the inimitable Philip Dick style. What is a Wub? A 400 pound slovenly, fat, ungainly, drooling animal that looks like a cross between a walrus and an enormous hog? Well, yes that is pretty much what he looks like and for 50 cents, a good bargain no matter how he tastes. The hungry spaceship crew expect to find out. Of course the Wub may not entirely agree but it doesn't have much to say about it. The second story, The Skull, is a skillful mesh of time travel, unscrupulous governments, prisoners, and religion. With an assassin thrown in for good measure. Enjoy! (summary by Phil Chenevert)

title: Beyond Good and Evil: Prelude to a Philosophy of the Future (Version 2) by Friedrich Nietzsche (1844
description:
By turns illuminating, infuriating, bewildering, and amusing, Nietzsche's masterwork covers a lot of ground. He rejects most strands of Western thought, especially on the subject of morality, and develops his own theme demanding that individuals embrace their own 'will to power' to give life intention and direction. First published in German in 1887, this translation was produced in 1907 by Helen Zimmern, a long-standing acquaintance of Nietzsche.

There is an accessible text at http://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/4363 but it leaves out all words in Greek, and is not in alignment with any printed edition; hence using the archive.org version. - Summary by Cori Samuel

title: Beyond Bedlam by Wyman Guin (1915 - 1989)
description:
However fantastic it may seem, the society
so elaborately described in this story has
its seeds in ours. Just check the data.... - Summary by Galaxy Science Fiction, August 1951

title: Beyond by John Galsworthy (1867 - 1933)
description:
Gyp, the daughter of ex-Major Charles Claire Winton, at the age of 23 marries Fiorsen, a Swedish violin virtuoso. Her mother, the wife of another man, has been Winton's mistress; she had died when Gyp was born. A highly sensitive child, Gyp has grown up in isolated surroundings with a kind, but very British, father.

As she gets older her father tries to introduce her into society. An attack of gout takes him to Wiesbaden for a cure and, as he never goes anywhere without her, she accompanies him. There she is mesmerised by Fiorsen and his playing. Intensely musical herself and inexperienced, she's flattered by the homage of the violinist although her father soon sees through him. She also sees he is talented but unstable, ignorant, vain, hypersensitive, with an air of romance – a typical virtuoso.

Gyp realises that she may well have made a mistake in marrying Fiorsen. The book follows what happens to her thereafter.
( Simon Evers)

title: Beware, The Usurpers! by Robert W. Krepps (1919 - 1980)
description: A sci-fi horror tale in which alien monsters skulk about in human form! William Chester and his comrades find themselves alone in being able to recognize this malevolence. Discover what happens when a handful of brave people pit their forces against the might of Earth's secret masters and the governments they control! You will find romance, action, and daring speculation in this lost classic.  - Summary by Adam Benckeser

title: Beverly Of Graustark by George Barr McCutcheon (1866 - 1928)
description:
Beverly Of Graustark is the second book in the Graustark series.
Lorry and his wife, the princess, made their home in Washington, but
spent a few months of each year in Edelweiss. During the periods spent
in Washington and in travel, her affairs in Graustark were in the hands
of a capable, austere old diplomat--her uncle, Count Caspar
Halfont. Princess Volga reigned as regent over the principality of
Axphain. To the south lay the principality of Dawsbergen, ruled by young
Prince Dantan, whose half brother, the deposed Prince Gabriel, had been
for two years a prisoner in Graustark, the convicted assassin of Prince
Lorenz, of Axphain, one time suitor for the hand of Yetive. (Project Gutenberg)

title: Between the Lines by Boyd Cable (1878 - 1943)
description: This book, all of which has been written at the Front within sound of the German guns and for the most part within shell and rifle range, is an attempt to tell something of the manner of struggle that has gone on for months between the lines along the Western Front, and more especially of what lies behind and goes to the making of those curt and vague terms in the war communiqués. I think that our people at Home will be glad to know more, and ought to know more, of what these bald phrases may actually signify, when, in the other sense, we read 'between the lines.' (Summary by Boyd Cable)

title: Audio - Adventures of Tom Sawyer
description: The classic story Adventures of Tom Sawyer on full audio. Never forget a book that made America a legend in print. Samual Clemins at his best!

title: Between the Larch-woods and the Weir by Flora Klickmann (1867 - 1958)
description: Flora Klickmann, a writer for the Girls' Own Paper and The Flower-Patch Among the Hills, brings us another charming volume, combining nature description, anecdote, autobiography, religion, and her own brand of humor in these observations based on her country cottage, neighbors and household. - Summary by Lynne Thompson

Welcome to the Chapter 25 Book Club Podcast. This is our bonus chapter, featuring exclusive conversations with authors.
Each of these episodes features an in-depth, hour-long interview, followed by a live question-and-answer session powered by our community members. From thrillers to romance, we cover a diverse range of genres, all delivered in the same gently welcoming tone. Chapter 25 is here to spark great conversations about books and authors, as we look for fresh perspectives and celebrate the joy of reading together.
Join us at https://www.chapter25bookclub.com/

title: Betty Wales, Freshman (version 2) by Margaret Warde (1875 - 1944)
description:
Betty Wales begins her time at Harding College. Her freshman year entails many misadventures. Betty Wales was a popular novel series that inspired a popular line of clothing. Many dresses came with a free book. - Summary by thechapterbrook

We discuss personal experiences of navigating awakenings, return to self, healing your body as your spiritual instrument, the power of water, earth magick, change makers, quantum physics, extraterrestrials, galactic heritage, religion v spirituality, education systems, government, false realities, synchronicities, breathwork, meditation, animal spirits, philosophy, culture...you name it, we're talking about it!

title: Bellweather Village: Cozy Mystery Sleep Stories
author: Lantern Key Studio
description:
Original cozy mystery sleep stories for adults.
In Bellweather Village, former archivist and bookshop owner Clara Vale follows small anomalies through rain-softened railway platforms, old hotels, harbour lanes, and warm village shops.
Each episode offers an emotionally safe mystery, rich sensory atmosphere, and a complete resolution as the village slowly settles toward night.
New episodes every week from Lantern Key Studio.

title: Betty Gordon at Bramble Farm by Alice B. Emerson
description:
Betty Gordon found herself an orphan at age twelve. She became the ward of her uncle, Richard Gordon. She immediately came to love her uncle dearly. Unfortunately, his job took him on travels regularly. Therefore, he decided to send Betty to spend the summer with an old friend, Agatha Peabody, at Bramble Farm. Little did Uncle Dick or Betty know, that Bramble Farm was not a welcoming or joyful place to live, as Agatha's husband was a miserly and sour man. The only bright spot in Betty's summer was her friendship with Bob, a poor orphan, bound to work tirelessly upon the farm. - Summary by Marie Christian

Alice B. Emerson is a house pseudonym used by the Stratemeyer Syndicate.

title: Betty Crocker's Bisquick Cook Book: 157 Recipes and Ideas by Betty Crocker
description: Betty Crocker, a creation of General Mills, has become synonymous with good American cooking and nearly every household had one of her cook books.   Here are 157 recipes featuring Bisquick, a popular pancake and baking mix created in 1931 by General Mills.  There is everything from coffee cake to dumplings. - Summary by Larry Wilson

title: Betty Baird's Ventures by Anna Hamlin Weikel (1865 - 1923)
description: Betty has graduated from the Pines and is now with her parents at their new home on Long Island. When Betty finds out that her father is having trouble paying the mortgage, she decides to spread her entrepreneurial wings and help with the family finances. But can a young girl in 1907 earn enough money to make a difference? Does Betty have the spirit and determination to stick with her plans, even when others try to discourage her? ( Holly Jenson)

title: Betty Baird's Golden Year by Anna Hamlin Weikel (1865 - 1923)
description: It seems that all the people close to Betty are going through major life changes. Lois is certainly spending a lot of time with Dunmore Lane these days. Is Betty about to lose her friend and confidante forever? Even Miss Minturne is behaving strangely. If she's falling in love, as Betty suspects, what will happen to the income that Betty is depending on to pay off the mortgage on the farmhouse? Meanwhile, before Betty is even aware of it, her friends have begun to wonder if someone is falling in love with her. Although Betty scoffs at the idea, there is a certain gentleman who just might fit her romantic ideal of a knight errant. But with so many young men smitten with Betty and her magnetic personality, which one, if any, will win her heart in the end? - Summary by Holly Jenson

Jonathan Swift, satirist, essayist, political pamphleteer (first for the Whigs, then for the Tories), poet and cleric who became Dean of St Patrick's Cathedral, Dublin. He is regarded by the Encyclopædia Britannica as the foremost prose satirist in the English language and is less well known for his poetry. - Summary by Wikipedia

title: The Bible Under the Microscope
author: Doc Roc
description:
What happens when a scientist examines the Bible with the same curiosity, discipline, and critical thinking used in scientific research?
Doc Roc combines his experience in science and theology to explore the Bible with clarity, insight, and practical relevance. Each episode examines passages, tackles life's big questions, and uncovers timeless truths that speak to the challenges of modern living.
Whether you're a lifelong believer, a curious seeker, or someone who enjoys thoughtful discussions about faith and reason, this podcast invites you to discover the Bible from a fresh perspective.

title: Betty Baird by Anna Hamlin Weikel (1865 - 1923)
description: This is the first book in the Betty Baird Series of boarding school books, a genre which was popular among young girls in the early 1900s. Our heroine, Betty Baird, who is herself obsessed with reading boarding school books, is sent away to The Pines, a boarding school where at first she is ridiculed by some of the wealthier and more popular students. As time goes on, Betty silences her foes with her unique and captivating personality, and she and her new friends have many lighthearted adventures during their days at The Pines. Anna Hamlin Wikel (pen name Weikel) was herself raised by a learned clergyman, Benjamin Baird Hamlin, upon whom the character of Betty Baird's father is based. The reader is the great grand niece of the author. (Holly Jenson)

title: Better Far to Pass Away by Richard Dennys (1884 - 1916)
description: At this time of year, we dedicate the Fortnightly Poetry project to the fallen in war. This poem, written at a time when the average life expectancy of an officer at the front was a mere six weeks, vividly demonstrates a young officer's expectation and acceptance of his own death. (Summary by Ruth Golding)

title: Betsey and I Are Out by Will Carleton (1845 - 1912)
description: LibriVox volunteers bring you 11 recordings of Betsey and I Are Out by Will Carleton. This was the fortnightly poetry project for March 22nd, 2009.

title: Betrothed (version 2 Dramatic Reading), The by Alessandro Manzoni (1785 - 1873)
description:
One of the most famous Italian books in history, The Betrothed was written by Alessandro Manzoni in 1827. The two main characters, Renzo and Lucy, are engaged to be married when Lucy tragically disappears. Little does Renzo know that Lucy has been kidnapped by a criminal, The Unnamed, feared by all the people of Northern Italy, where the story is set. Despite the threat of death and the fear of never laying eyes on one another again, Renzo and Lucy maintain a deep love for each other throughout the book that displays the true sacrifice and commitment of marriage. The book was recommended by Pope Francis to engaged couples in 2015 as preparation for marriage. - Summary by Woolly Bee

title: The Dapper Duo: A Disney Podcast
author: Frank and Emily Shaffer
description: Welcome to The Dapper Duo: a podcast where husband-and-wife Disney duo, Frank and Emily, spread Disney magic one episode at a time. From the parks to the screen and all the magic in between, we share fun conversations, favorite memories, hidden details, trip tips, Disney history, movies, music, food, and the stories that make Disney so special. title: Republic of Hate
author: republicofhate
description:
Iran’s deep-seated anti-Semitism has persisted for over four decades. With the rise of the Islamic revolution, hidden anti-Semitic sentiments surged to levels unseen in generations. A long-dormant hatred of Jews resurfaced. After seizing power in 1979, Iran’s new leader, Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini, reversed the Shah’s pro-Western policies. The once-prominent museum exhibitions and art-house cinema in Tehran disappeared. Non-Muslim Iranians became suspects in the eyes of the newly formed Iranian intelligence and security services.

In the mid-1980s, Iranian state-controlled media outlets began vilifying Jews. Illustrators of children’s cartoons depicted Jews as pigs and apes. Themes in television talk shows and literary coffee klatches include Jewish vampirism and old blood libels. Clergy recycled ancient accusations of Jews persecuting Muhammad. Among politicians and public intellectuals, the word "Jew" is used as a slur in Iranian political discourse. The words "Jew" and "Zionist" are often used interchangeably and generally with disdain. This podcast explores the nature of impact of this hatred.

title: The Perceptive Zeal Podcast
author: perceptivezeal
description:
Zealous on the journey of cultivating personal growth & development - Writing & sharing unique thoughts and perspectives. 
This Podcast will be less than 30 minutes per day – daily Bible reading about 2 pages from my under 700-page copy of this Bible, so the Podcast will be completed in less than 1 year. We’ll record the reading, then do a separate short commentary on what stood out and what I’m reflecting on. 
Free download page link: https://perceptivezeal.com/podcast/ 
*(no gated requirements, downloads to PDF format to open in a web browser)
Paperback copies on Amazon.

title: THE BELLADONA WITCH
author: The Belladona Witch
description: Welcome to Belladonna After Dark. I'm your anonymous host. We're here for witchcraft, folklore, dark humor, and the kind of life stories that make you laugh now because crying got old.

author: BETSI ROMERO
description:

description:

From lucid dreams and out-of-body experiences to meditation, healing, and consciousness itself, this podcast explores the extraordinary experiences that can transform the way we see ourselves and the world around us.

title: Betrothed (I Promessi Sposi), The by Alessandro Manzoni (1785 - 1873)
description:
The Betrothed (I Promessi Sposi) presents a kaleidoscope of individual stories, which are all tied together by the story of Lucia and Renzo, two young persons of humble origin that are deeply in love with one another. However, despite their great attachment, they are prevented from marrying by the cruel Don Rodrigo, who has himself cast an eye on the beautiful and pious Lucia. Don Rodrigo menaces the priest who was to perform the wedding ceremony, who then refuses to do his duty. Thus threatened and prevented from being married, the couple is separated, and the narration follows each of them on their struggle to unite again.

Set in the years 1628-1630, the main story is accompanied by a rich and most accurate description of the historical background -- the warfaring cities, the plague, and the oppressive Spanish rule over Northern Italy during that time. The narration also includes many smaller subplots which allow the reader glimpses into the lives of the other characters involved.

Alessandro Manzoni is regarded as one of the most important authors of Italian literature, and this particular work is counted to the overall most important literary works of the 19th century.

The adventures of Tyltyl and The Fairy Berylune. Of all the girls he knows, who does he love the most? Read on to find out. - Summary by Michele Eaton

title: Bethlehem by Frederick William Faber (1814 - 1863)
description:
There are several ways in which we may treat of the mysteries of the Three-and-Thirty Years of our dearest Lord. We may look at each of them singly, as it is in itself, full of grace and beauty, and distinctively unlike any other. Secondly, we may gather them up into departments, and call them the joyful, the sorrowful, and the glorious mysteries, the three sets differing thus from each other, and, in the unity of each set, each mystery having its own distinctness. Or, thirdly, we may view them as clustering in constellations, and yet these constellations unities, as the Childhood, the Hidden Life, the Public Ministry, the Passion, and the Risen Life or Great Forty Days. Each of these constellations has a more perfect unity than the divisions of mysteries according to their joyous, sorrowful, or glorious character, while at the same time the single mysteries, which compose the unities, have also a greater variety. Fourthly, we have much to learn by putting out of view the separate mysteries, and studying the contrasts and comparisons of those five constellations one with another. It is hard to say whether their analogies or diversities are the most full of theology and devotion.

The following Treatise is a specimen of the third method of considering the Thirty-Three Years, united, where it was naturally suggested, with the fourth. In my own mind, probably from a poetical habit of localizing things, I have become accustomed to know those Five Constellations of Mysteries by the names of Bethlehem, Nazareth, Galilee, Calvary, and Gennesareth, names which will be seen at once to be only approximately true, yet sufficiently so for my purpose.

I must also warn you, and through you my readers, that there are parts of the Treatise liable to be misunderstood without the reading of the whole. In all other respects it will explain itself, and I confide it to your indulgence and theirs, praying our Blessed Lord, if He sees fit, to allow it to quicken and brighten the fires of Christmas in child-like hearts. (Summary by Prefatory Epistle)

title: Bethink Yourselves! by Leo Tolstoy (1828 - 1910)
description: As Russia goes to war against Japan, Tolstoy urges those at all levels of society, from the Tsar down to the common soldier, to consider their actions in the light of Christ's teaching. "However strange this may appear, the most effective and certain deliverance of men from all the calamities which they inflict upon themselves and from the most dreadful of all—war—is attainable, not by any external general measures, but merely by that simple appeal to the consciousness of each separate man which, nineteen hundred years ago, was proposed by Jesus—that every man bethink himself, and ask himself, who is he, why he lives, and what he should and should not do." (Introduction by David Barnes, and extract from Chapter VI)

title: Beth Gêlert, or the Grave of the Greyhound by William Robert Spencer (1770 - 1834)
description:
LibriVox volunteers bring you 16 recordings of Beth Gêlert, or the Grave of the Greyhound by William Robert Spencer. This was the Fortnightly Poetry project for July 8, 2012.

William Robert Spencer, English poet and wit, was the younger son of Lord Charles Spencer and his wife Mary Beauclerk. Spencer's wit made him a popular member of society. He belonged to the Whig set of Charles James Fox and Richard Brinsley Sheridan and was frequently a guest of the prince of Wales. He did not desire a public life, being content as a writer of "occasional" verse and vers de société.
His writings were greatly appreciated by his contemporaries, being warmly praised by such figures as Sir Walter Scott, John Wilson, and Lord Byron. ( Summary from Wikipedia )

title: Women Who Ain’t Afraid to Curse When Communicating with God
author: Trelani Michelle
description: The official companion podcast to the book of the same name! In these short voice notes, Trelani shares stories, reflections, journal prompts, and meditations on intuition, creativity, release, boundaries, pleasure, and spiritual liberation in the language she actually speaks to God. Honest, heartfelt, and sometimes with a lil cussin, these conversations invite you to trust yourself, laugh a little, heal a little, and remember that your WHOLE self is always welcome in the conversation.

title: Best Russian Short Stories by Various
description:
In this collection of Russian stories, editor and compiler Thomas Seltzer selects from a range of the best examples of 19th and early 20th century Russian literature. As a survey of famous authors at the height of the powers, as well as some writers who have been unjustly neglected, this anthology is indispensable. (Summary by Nullifidian)

"Everything is subordinated to two main requirements--humanitarian ideals and fidelity to life. This is the secret of the marvellous simplicity of Russian literary art."--THOMAS SELTZER.

title: Best Nonsense Verses, The by Josephine Daskam Bacon (1876 - 1961)
description: From Lewis Carroll's Jabberwocky to limericks written by Anonymous, some of the crankiest, most logical and lyrical people turn common sense upside-down. May they inspire the child inside of you to find your way through the most challenging situations with a new set of eyes! Josephine Dodge Daskam, aka Josephine Daskam Bacon, selected these nonsense verses with the permission of their authors Lewis Carroll, Edward Lear, W.S. Gilbert, Guy Wetmore Carryl, Charles E. Carryl, Oliver Herford, Gelett Burgess, George du Maurier, and Rudyard Kipling. (Grace Buchanan)

From the early influential tales of Daniel Defoe and Edward Bulwer-Lytton to more contemporary masters of the terror tale such as M. R. James, Algernon Blackwood and E. F. Benson, this collection brings together a bounty of riches sure to keep you awake into the dark, dreadful night! - Summary by Ben Tucker

title: The Shadow In The Light
author: Anna Ebert

Sometimes the moments that change your life are the ones you never see coming.


The Shadow in the Light is an original audio drama exploring memory, connection, hope, and the choices that shape us. Through compelling characters, moments of humour, and emotionally driven stories, the series blends intimate drama with thoughtful science fiction, inviting listeners to question what it means to be human and to find light even in the darkest moments.

Elke aflevering draait om herkenbare situaties, nieuwe inzichten en onderwerpen die je laten nadenken of motiveren. Soms luchtig en grappig, soms diepgaand en serieus, maar altijd oprecht en toegankelijk. Of je nu onderweg bent, ontspant of inspiratie zoekt: deze podcast is een plek waar je even kan luisteren, leren en jezelf herkennen in echte verhalen en gesprekken.

The texts are preceded by two essays concerning hymns and their standards.  Benson (1855-1930) was an American Presbyterian minister who edited several hymnals.  - Summary by David Wales

Twenty-four short stories by famous and not-so-famous British authors. (Summary by David Wales)

title: Exterior to the Evidence: A Gripping Mystery on the Moors
description: In the remote moors, a puzzling death sets off a chain of events that leads to hidden secrets and unexpected twists. J. S. Fletcher's 'Exterior to the Evidence' immerses listeners in a captivating mystery where a missing will raises questions about loyalty and greed. As the story unfolds, several suspects emerge, each with their own motives and alibis, keeping you guessing until the very end. The tension builds as the investigator delves deeper into the lives of those connected to the deceased, exposing dark truths and tangled relationships. With rich descriptions and a gripping plot, this audiobook invites you to piece together the clues alongside the characters. Discover the intricacies of human nature and the lengths people will go to protect their interests in this classic tale of crime and intrigue. Perfect for fans of detective fiction, 'Exterior to the Evidence' offers both thrills and thought-provoking insights, making it a must-listen for anyone who loves a good whodunit.

title: Bessie's Fortune by Mary Jane Holmes (1825 - 1907)
description: The coverup of a murder leads to the search for the dead man's heiress decades later in this delightful 3 part novel by Mary Jane Holmes. Her characters are as always very interesting and realistic. Poor little Bessie is raised in poverty because her mother spends most of her time at the gaming tables of Monte Carlo or Baden Baden while her so called aristocratic father looks down on all menial work. Her wealthy relatives will do nothing to help.  Add the love of three men for one girl to the mix and the result is an absorbing historical romance. - Summary by Celine Major

title: Bertrams, The by Anthony Trollope (1815 - 1882)
description: This is a massive effort, taking place in England and the Middle East, with a cast of thousands... Well, not thousands, fortunately, but certainly a great many. There are three Bertrams present: Sir Lionel, his son George (our hero), and the wealthy uncle. The primary story involves the joining of two couples (following some rocky periods), and the disposal of the uncle's fortune. The more amusing bits focus on peripheral characters in the social settings of a fictional town and in travel on board ship or touring Egypt. Trollope digresses from time to time to lecture us on religion, politics, and eccentricities of the British tourist. (Summary by Arnold Banner)

title: Bertram Cope's Year by Henry Blake Fuller (1857 - 1929)
description:
This novel was perhaps the most daring and affirmative LGBT literature of the first two decades of the 20th century in America. In this story, Bertram Cope is a young college instructor, about twenty-four years old ("certainly not a day over twenty-five"), who is pursued by men and women, both younger and older than himself. In writing this novel, Fuller had to carefully craft his plot schemes so as not to offend the sensibilities of publishers. As a result, today's reader is left somewhat, but not entirely, confused about the precise feelings that characters develop for one another by the end of the book. Nevertheless, no publishing house would touch it, which ultimately required that Fuller go the self-publishing route.

Bertram Cope's Year was the first novel by an established American writer that dealt exclusively with the subject of homosexuality. Although it did not achieve the notariety it deserved at the time, it set the tone for the increasingly open discussion of gay themes in literary form. (Introduction by James K. White)

title: Bert's Treatise of Hawkes and Hawking by Edmund Bert ( - fl.1619)
description:
An approved Treatise of Hawkes and Hawking. Divided into three Bookes. The first teacheth, How to make a short-winged Hawke good, with good conditions. The second, How to reclaime a Hawke from any ill condition. The third teacheth. Cures for all knowne griefes and diseases. By Edmund Bert, Gentleman.

The author, who lived at Collier Row, near Romford, Essex, details his method of training the Goshawk, in which he was very successful. He used to ride out of Essex into Sussex to hawk over the downs, where, he says, " I have killed for the most part of a moneth together with an intermewed goshawke eight, nine, or tenne Partridges in a day. The day of my going thither and the day of my returne to London was just five weeks, and it was a fortnight or more in Michaelmas terme when I came backe. I killed in that time with that one hawke foure score and odd Partridges, five Pheasants, seven Rayles, and four Hares against my will."

He had " for a Goshawke and Tarsell a hundred marks both solde to one man within sixteen months ". For another Goshawk he was offered " fortie pounds ", and ultimately sold her for thirty. In 1795, a silver " varvel," engraved with the name "Oxenforde," was found near Headingham Castle, the ancient seat of this family in Essex. It is figured and described in the Archceologia, and may well have belonged to the nobleman to whom this book is dedicated. - Summary by James Edmund Harting, 1891.

title: Bert Wilson at the Wheel by J. W. Duffield (1859 - 1946)
description: First volume of an adventure series for young adults - Summary by kathrinee

title: The Window at the White Cat: A Lawyer's Dilemma
description: In the heart of a bustling city lies The White Cat, a back-street political club where secrets brew alongside beers. Meet a well-meaning but clumsy lawyer who unexpectedly finds himself entangled with a pair of charming old maids and a captivating young woman. As he navigates this quirky trio, he realizes he must adopt the skills of his sleuthing friends, a detective and a sharp-witted newspaperman, to unravel a mystery that mixes political favors with the threat of murder. This engaging tale by Mary Roberts Rinehart weaves humor and intrigue, inviting listeners into a world where every character has a story and every clue leads deeper into the unknown. Join him as he discovers that solving puzzles is not just about logic but also about understanding the human heart. With twists and turns at every corner, this audiobook promises to keep you guessing until the very end.

title: Under Warranty
author: qbwc.com
description:
Under Warranty is a podcast for home builders and professionals in the residential construction industry.
Hosted by the team at Quality Builders Warranty (qbwc.com), the US's premier provider of 10-year new home structural warranties serving builders for more than 40 years.
Hear stories from the field, interviews with leaders in the industry, best practices, and strategy about protecting reputations for the short and long term. The discussions are practical, straightforward, and rooted in real experience.

title: The Wellness Table Podcast (TWTP)
description:
New podcast weblog 

The Wellness Table’s Discussion topics about:

  • Nutrition 
  • Religion/Bible
  • Health
  • Fitness
  • Education
  • General Topics

title: The AWHONN Labor Lens with Lisa Miller
author: AWHONN
description: The Labor Lens with Lisa Miller explores the complexities of perinatal care through expert conversations, real clinical cases, and practical insights. Each episode brings together diverse perspectives and evidence-informed discussion to support nurses and clinicians dedicated to improving outcomes for women and babies.

title: Bad Critics
author: Blake Melanson & Chuck Corder
description:
Chuck and Blake don’t simply disagree about which films are good. They disagree about what films are FOR. Chuck believes movies are a craft to be assessed — their quality is a function of intentionality, architecture, and whether they earn the emotional or intellectual response they're reaching for. Blake believes movies are an experience to be felt — their quality is a function of whether something real moved through him when the lights went down. Both positions are philosophically defensible. Neither host can fully respect the other’s framework without abandoning his own. That irreducible tension is every episode.


Welcome to Bad Critics!

title: Beric the Briton: a Story of the Roman Invasion by G. A. Henty (1832 - 1902)
description: My series of stories dealing with the wars of England would be altogether incomplete did it not include the period when the Romans were the masters of the country. The valour with which the natives of this island defended themselves was acknowledged by the Roman historians, and it was only the superior discipline of the invaders that enabled them finally to triumph over the bravery and the superior physical strength of the Britons. The Roman conquest for the time was undoubtedly of immense advantage to the people -- who had previously wasted their energies in perpetual tribal wars -- as it introduced among them the civilization of Rome. In the end, however, it proved disastrous to the islanders, who lost all their military virtues. Having been defended from the savages of the north by the soldiers of Rome, the Britons were, when the legions were recalled, unable to offer any effectual resistance to the Saxons, who, coming under the guise of friendship, speedily became their masters, imposing a yoke infinitely more burdensome than that of Rome, and erasing almost every sign of the civilization that had been engrafted upon them. How far the British population disappeared under the subsequent invasion and the still more oppressive yoke of the Danes is uncertain; but as the invaders would naturally desire to retain the people to cultivate the land for them, it is probable that the great mass of the Britons were not exterminated. It is at any rate pleasant to believe that with the Saxon, Danish, and Norman blood in our veins, there is still a large admixture of that of the warriors who fought so bravely against Caesar, and who rose under Boadicea in a desperate effort to shake off the oppressive rule of Rome.. (Introduction by G.A.Henty)

Living With Now goes behind the practice and into the lives of the healers themselves. What led them there? A mother's breakdowns. A childhood escape from a war zone. Expect culture, music, memory, art, fringe ideas, conspiracy, spirituality, absurdity, old friendships, new ghosts, and the occasional detour into whatever rabbit hole is open that week.

The original show was live, loose, intimate, and a little feral. This reboot keeps that spirit, now broadcasting from northern Portugal with the same basic idea: real conversations, strange signals, no corporate polish.

title: Sew with Confidence | Sewing, Fabric, Sewing Patterns, Sewing with Knits
author: Lisa Hawkes - Pattern Designer & Sewing Teacher
description:
Do you want to sew clothes but are worried that the clothes will look homemade?  Are you afraid of wasting your time, money & fabric? Tired of sewing beautiful clothes that never actually get worn, but just hang in the back of the closet?  Do you want to expand your sewing skills but are nervous about trying new techniques? 
 Welcome to Sew with Confidence! This podcast will help expand your sewing skills, provide guidance in choosing the materials for your next project, and help you confidently sew clothes that you and others will love to wear!
 Hi, I’m Lisa, the host of Sew with Confidence and the owner and designer behind Pink Hollybush Designs, a line of sewing patterns for children’s garments. I have been sewing since I was a little girl and my background is in smocking and heirloom sewing. I’ve had my share of sewing fails - garments that didn’t fit, choosing the wrong fabric, and sewing pieces that just weren’t my style or appropriate for my life. I hate the thought of the time and money that has been wasted.
 Through trial and error, along with many sewing classes, I have figured out how to sew with confidence. I discovered that choosing the right fabric and sewing techniques can make all the difference between a garment looking homemade or being one that you’re proud to wear.  
 If you are ready to expand your sewing skills, confidently sew clothes that you and others will love to wear, and be part of a supportive sewing community, then fire up that sewing machine, dive into your fabric stash, join me, and let’s Sew with Confidence!

Break the patterns you can’t see -by understanding the energy beneath them.
The Pattern Breaker Podcast explores the energetic patterns, subconscious loops, and emotional imprints shaping our lives. 
Blending psychology, intuition, and energetic awareness, each episode helps you recognize the deeper dynamics behind repeating situations, reactions, and relationships. When you begin to see the energetic architecture beneath your life, patterns lose their grip and real change becomes possible.

title: The Virtue Signal

description: We commonly use the term “virtue signaling” to refer to an outward gesture of “virtue” that is meant to win approval from an audience as opposed to an action arising out of authentic personal integrity. In The Virtue Signal, host Rhonda McDaniel looks past applause-winning gestures to engage with the timeless foundations of personal character and demonstrate the importance of virtue for personal growth in character and integrity as well as for citizens in a self-governing society.

description:
Welcome to Rancho Cordova Utility Insights, a high-authority audio playbook engineered for premium residential property protection, mechanical line troubleshooting, and strategic asset maintenance. Hosted by the technical specialists at Affordable Plumbing Solutions, this channel delivers professional architectural commentary designed to help local homeowners decode complex utility crises before they cause expensive structural degradation.

From deep main line flow anomalies and water heating system overhauls to sudden midnight pipe bursts, we address real-world engineering failures with upfront diagnostic solutions. When an active emergency threatens your floors or electrical grids, relying on delayed responses or uncertified handymen can compromise your entire real estate investment. Connecting with a trusted 24 hour plumber rancho cordova dispatcher guarantees that licensed technicians armed with industrial-grade diagnostic tools deploy to your property instantly, safeguarding your infrastructure regardless of the hour. Tune into our weekly episodes to optimize your home preservation strategies, master preventative utility checklists, and keep your property systems operating with absolute operational integrity.

title: Prepared for Anything: The FlareSyn

description:
Emergencies happen without warning, and the difference between panic and preparedness often comes down to knowledge and the right equipment. Prepared for Anything is a podcast dedicated to helping first responders, outdoor enthusiasts, tactical professionals, and everyday citizens develop the confidence and skills needed to respond effectively during medical emergencies.

Each episode features practical discussions on trauma care, bleeding control, emergency planning, survival strategies, gear selection, and real-world lessons from experienced medics and preparedness experts. Whether you're assembling your first emergency kit or refining your existing setup, you'll gain actionable insights that can make a meaningful difference when every second counts.

For listeners looking to better understand lifesaving bleeding-control equipment, explore FlareSyn's collection of tactical tourniquets, designed to complement comprehensive emergency preparedness and trauma response kits.

From wilderness adventures and road trips to workplace safety and everyday carry, this series empowers listeners with practical education, evidence-based guidance, and a preparedness mindset that extends far beyond the gear itself. Every episode reinforces one simple mission: preparation today can save lives tomorrow.

description:
Audacity of Entrepreneurs is a podcast about what it actually takes to build something from scratch, and survive the process.

Every episode dives deep into the journeys of founders, operators, and creators who chose to take risks when playing safe was easier. We go beyond highlight reels and vanity metrics to unpack the real mechanics behind building companies: the messy beginnings, the wrong bets, the pivots, and the breakthroughs that changed everything.

This podcast focuses on:

  • Decision-making under uncertainty - how founders think when there’s no clear path
  • Execution over ideas - what separates builders from talkers
  • Failure analysis - what went wrong, and what it taught them
  • Growth systems - content, distribution, hiring, and scaling strategies
  • Founder psychology - discipline, obsession, burnout, and resilience

You’ll hear unfiltered conversations about:

  • First customers and early traction
  • Scaling from 0 to meaningful revenue
  • Building teams, culture, and systems
  • Navigating competition, doubt, and pressure
  • The trade-offs no one warns you about

If you’re an entrepreneur, creator, or operator trying to build something meaningful, this podcast gives you real-world insight, not recycled advice.

No clichés. No surface-level motivation.
Just honest conversations with people who’ve done the work.

author: Dr Simone Sleep
description:
On Monday 30th June 2026 One Health Oral Health Australia facilitated a one-day Oral Parasites Workshop as an adjunct to the 2026 ASP Annual Conference at the Mantra on View Surfers Paradise. 

This workshop’s motivation was to bring together dentistry, parasitology, microbiology and public health to revisit a question that has sat at the edge of oral health science for decades: what is the true significance of parasites in the oral cavity?

Keynote Speakers included:
Dr Simone Sleep
Associate Professor Lotti Tajouri
Dr Mark Bonner
Susan Rusalen
Tylen Burt 
And more

This series of podcasts are the audio recordings of their individual presentations.

author: tim li
description:
Welcome to The Tarot Club Archives, your premier late-night podcast dedicated to decoding the cosmic horrors, intricate magic systems, and historical blueprints of modern progression fantasy milestones.
Hosted as a detailed, chapter-by-chapter literary deep dive, this show dismantles the complex layers of "Lord of the Mysteries" (诡秘之主) by Cuttlefish That Loves Diving, alongside other genre-defining web fiction masterpieces. We step beyond the gas-lit streets and heavy smog to analyze everything from the heartbreaking socio-economics of working-class families to the divine, mind-bending bluffs performed above the Gray Fog.
We don't just recap plots—we dissect the anatomy of world-building, trace the hidden pathways of potions and sequences, and question whether the cosmic laws are a sanctuary or an existential trap. Whether you are a veteran Beyonder or a curious traveler stepping into the fog for the very first time, dim the lights, grab your tea, and join our weekly archives.
Praise the Fool. New episodes drop weekly.

title: Francis Chan Sermons
author: Francis Chan
description: Francis Chan is an american pastor inspired with the word of God to lighten up his generation Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Joy Minimum
author: That Quirky Bird Girl
description:
If you've been on your joy journey, buckle up, because the ride is about to get wild! Join Steph (known online as That Quirky Bird Girl) on her mission to expand joy as far and wide as the eye can reach. 
Each week, we sit down with joy entrepreneurs who are doing their darndest to find joy through grief, happiness, sadness, and everything that life throws at us in between!

title: StoryWagen
author: StoryWagen
description: A podcast where we talk about grief, moral injury, and other spiritual health issues.

title: In the Yo with King Vito
author: King Vito
description: An intimate conversation with your favorite artist and groups on their biggest songs and greatest albums!

title: Mr. Creeps: Scary Stories & Creepypastas
author: Mr. Creeps
description: Everybody loves a good scary story, but what if some of those stories turn out to be true? I narrate creepypastas and other horror stories. Perfect for those late nights and rainy days. Sit back and try not to get too scared.

title: Shooting the Breese: Powered by ASW
author: Thomas Hakim
description:
Shooting the Breese: Powered by ASW is built on straightforward conversations with people who know the industry from the inside out. The conversations focus on what it really takes to build teams, run businesses, and adapt to change in today’s professional landscape.

 

Each episode captures unscripted sessions where guests share personal experiences, insights, and challenges from their careers. The discussions cover leadership, operations, people management, and how businesses are responding to new expectations and evolving market pressures. There is no gloss and no sales pitch, just honest dialogue.

 

With the support of AS White Global, conversations touch on global workforce models and how businesses are rethinking productivity and support structures. Daniel’s relaxed approach creates a conversational atmosphere that feels more like a roundtable discussion than a traditional interview.

 

Shooting the Breese is for professionals who appreciate realism, practical thinking, and hearing how others have navigated similar challenges. Whether you are leading a team or building your next phase of growth, this podcast offers insights grounded in experience.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The local quantum conspiracy’s Podcast
author: localquantumconspiracy
description: Hello and welcome to the local quantum conspiracy podcast where we cover everything from UAP's to the paranormal.

title: Christ Centered Sexuality Podcast
author: SJ
description:
The Christ-Centered Sexuality Podcast is a formation-focused, pastoral expression of Christ-Centered Sexuality (CCS). In a cultural moment marked by confusion, fear, and polarization around sex, sexuality, gender, marriage, and singleness, many Christians feel caught between conviction and compassion. This podcast exists to form people within that tension—not inflame it—offering biblical clarity, thoughtful conversation, and pastoral wisdom to help you follow Jesus with courage, humility, and care.
Welcome to the digital living room...pull up a chair. I’m really glad you’re here.

title: Titans Latest | Daily Tennessee Titans Podcast
author: Fan Arch
description:
Titans Latest is the daily Tennessee Titans podcast from the Fan Arch Network, bringing fans comprehensive coverage of everything happening with the two-tone blue. Whether it's breaking news, roster moves, free agency, draft developments, training camp battles, game previews, postgame reactions, or in-depth analysis, Titans Latest keeps you connected to the stories that matter most.

Each episode delivers timely insights, discussion, and coverage of the biggest headlines surrounding the Titans, while also diving into player performances, coaching decisions, AFC South rivalries, and the team's path toward contention. From offseason speculation to the regular season grind, Titans Latest provides year-round coverage designed for passionate Titans fans who want to stay informed every day.

If you're looking for the latest Tennessee Titans news, analysis, rumors, and opinions all in one place, Titans Latest is your daily destination for everything Titans football.

title: Beyond the Ordinary
author: Charlese Ambers
description:
Beyond the Ordinary is the podcast for people who know they were meant for more.

More freedom.
More purpose.
More impact.
More adventure.
More life.


Hosted by mental health therapist and entrepreneur Charlese Ambers, Beyond the Ordinary explores the powerful intersection of mental health, entrepreneurship, identity, and human potential.


Because building an extraordinary life isn't just about strategy.

It's about overcoming fear.
Breaking free from limiting beliefs.
Trusting yourself when no one else understands the vision.
And developing the emotional resilience to keep going when comfort is calling your name.


Through honest conversations, inspiring stories, expert interviews, and practical action steps, you'll hear from entrepreneurs, creators, professionals, and everyday people who dared to challenge the status quo and pursue something greater.

If you've ever felt called to a bigger life but struggled with self-doubt, anxiety, perfectionism, fear of failure, or the opinions of others, you're in the right place.

title: Inner Lives
author: Mariana Crestani
description:
Inner Lives invites you into the rich inner worlds of modern women. 

Through intimate conversations and reflective storytelling, Mariana Crestani explores identity, ambition, motherhood, relationships, aging, and reinvention. This show offers language for the questions we quietly live with and remind us that there’s no one right way to live a meaningful life.

title: 'LEVEL UP' Podcast
author: Ross Brown
description:
The LEVEL UP Podcast

Hosted by Ross Brown


This is a show for the disciplined,  not the distracted.


Each week, we sit down with real people doing real work, entrepreneurs, leaders, athletes, and creators who are levelling up with serious intent in their field.


We talk money, mindset, failure, and focus.

No hype. No bullshit. Just honest conversations from people building what actually matters.


If you’re tired of surface-level success stories and ready for the unfiltered truth behind what it really takes,  this is the podcast for you.

title: Mama Juliana Toronto Food Reviews
author: Mama Juliana
description: Mama Juliana is a Toronto-based food review podcast where two everyday food lovers break down the city’s dining scene through the abuela’s view - honest, detailed, and always real. From food and cocktails to ambiance, service, and the full experience, we dive into it all through fun, energetic conversations.

title: Tiny Big Quiz | The Weekly Quiz Podcast
author: Tiny Big Quiz - The Pocket Size Trivia Card Game
description:
The Tiny Big Quiz Podcast is the fast, fun weekly quiz you can play anywhere.
Every Friday, join host Neil Bentley as he rolls the dice and brings you 10 questions chosen live from the official Tiny Big Quiz app. From history and geography to movies, music, sport, science and everything in between - every episode is different!
Whether you’re on the school run, commuting, walking the dog or looking for a family quiz night in your pocket, you’ve got 30 seconds to answer each question and see how many points you can score.
🔗 www.tinybigquiz.com >> Use Code: POD10 for 10% off

title: Unlocking More
author: Brittany McWilliams
description: Your trusted guide to Indianapolis real estate. Brittany McWilliams sits down with local experts and real clients to share the stories and strategies you need to buy, sell, or invest with confidence. Expect practical advice, community insights, and a focus on what matters most to you. Let’s unlock more together in Indy!

title: Faith Wins
author: Chad Connelly
description:
Faith Wins with Chad Connelly is a podcast centered on faith, leadership, and the role pastors play in shaping communities and culture across America. Hosted by Chad Connelly, the show features conversations with pastors, faith leaders, and influential voices who are leading with conviction, purpose, and a Biblical worldview.
Chad brings decades of leadership experience to the microphone. A Clemson University engineering graduate, former US Army Armor officer, business leader, former South Carolina GOP Chairman, and the Republican National Committee's first-ever National Director of Faith Engagement, Chad has spent years speaking to pastors and faith communities nationwide. Through Faith Wins, he continues his mission to inspire, inform, and equip faith leaders to engage culture and lead boldly in today's world, because when pastors lead, faith wins.

title: The Soft Revolution Podcast
author: Siri Birk
description:
The Soft Revolution Podcast is a weekly drop in hosted by somatic coach, artist, and entrepreneur Siri Birk.
An exploration on creative expression, emotional liberation, embodied leadership, and living a life rooted in generosity, reciprocity, and reverence for the Earth and the path of choosing authenticity over efficiency, every day. 
Welcome to the Soft Revolution!

title: Murder of Words on AAC
author: Chelsea Hagen
description:
Hi, I’m Chelsea Hagen — creator of Noggin Art by Chelsea, host of Murder of Words on AAC, English student, artist, writer, and proud owner of a sexy robot voice.

I live in Alberta, where I create art with my brain, write too many words for my degree, and use an AAC device with two head switches to communicate. I’m powered by curiosity, dark humour, and the firm belief that crows and sarcasm are both highly underrated.

Welcome to my little corner of the internet — where art, stories, podcasts, and occasional chaos all live together.

title: The Crevice: The Mystery of a Financier's Fall
description: When Pennington Lawton, a wealthy financier, dies unexpectedly from what seems like a heart attack, his daughter Anita is left in shock. With the revelation that her father was on the brink of bankruptcy, Anita finds herself nearly destitute and desperate for answers. Determined to uncover the truth behind her father's death and the sudden loss of their fortune, she hires renowned detective Henry Blaine. As Blaine delves into the case, he uncovers a web of deceit and hidden motives that leads to unexpected twists and shocking revelations. Will Anita find the closure she seeks, or will the truth prove more dangerous than she ever imagined? 'Crevice' is a gripping detective fiction that explores themes of family loyalty, betrayal, and the lengths one will go to uncover the truth. Join Henry Blaine as he navigates dark secrets and complex relationships in a race against time to bring justice to a family torn apart by tragedy.

title: Struggle Bus
author: Struggle Bus
description: Nick Viagas and Ivy Wolk's podcast

title: Tape & Testimony
author: Joshua Pfortmiller
description:
Behind every case, there is a record.

Tape & Testimony features real police interrogations, interviews, witness statements, and courtroom testimony from some of the most famous and compelling criminal cases. Through official recordings and public record materials, each episode takes you inside the moments where stories change, pressure builds, and the truth begins to surface.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: CTW's Movies that Made Us
author: Chasing the Whimsy
description:
Old Man Ben and his friends Lasagna and Hammer as they revisit the movies that shaped them—emotionally, creatively, and sometimes questionably. From the cartoons that sparked their love of animation, to the anime that blew their minds, to the scrappy indie films of the 90s and the comedies that walked the line between absurdity and brilliance, nothing is off the table.
Each episode, they watch, debate, and celebrate the films that left a mark on their lives. Whether you’re here for nostalgia, discovery, or just to hear three friends passionately disagree, pull up a seat. Join the convo.

title: All-New Everyday Agenda With LHMusic & Friends
description: It’s all about the everyday things I do in my agenda like Omaha Symphonic Chorus, Wednesday Night Choir Rehearsal at church, Voluntary work at the VA Hospital, go to coffee shops like 13th Street Coffee or Sozo Coffeehouse & so much more on my new podcast every Weekend.

title: The Lanier Collection
author: Josh Dower
description:
Welcome to The Lanier Collection Podcast, your premier source for navigating luxury real estate on North Lake Lanier. Join us as we decode the waterfront market, from dock permits to finding your dream lakeside estate. 
Whether you’re an investor or buying your forever home, we provide the insights needed to master the Georgia lake lifestyle. Don’t settle for less—elevate your search today. 
Explore exclusive property portfolios and expert market advice at https://thelaniercollection.com/

title: Licensed to Overthink
author: Brittany Sperry and Becca Smith
description:
Welcome to Licensed to Overthink: Anxiety and Other Hobbies—the podcast for people whose brains are always just a little too committed to the assignment.
If you've ever replayed a conversation, overanalyzed a text message, worried about something that hasn't happened yet, or wondered whether everyone else has life figured out, you're our people.
Join us each week as we explore anxiety, mental health, relationships, resilience, and the wonderfully weird ways our minds work. With a blend of professional insight, personal experience, and plenty of humor, we're here to remind you that you're not the only one overthinking it.
And no, that thing you said five years ago probably wasn't weird.

title: Season 2 Episode 4 Tool Time
author: Matthew Chanler Marc Gaskin
description: Season 2 Episode 4 Tool Time

title: Challenger Teaches Me: League of Legends Champion Guides
author: We Teach League
description: Challenger Teaches Me is a League of Legends show where a Challenger player teaches host Nathan Mott how to play one champion per episode. We cover the best build and runes, live practice tool mechanics, a deep strategy interview, and a full VOD review of a real Challenger game. We also expose the common mistakes and community myths that keep most players stuck on that champion. Every episode closes with a matchup tier list from hardest to easiest. Brought to you by We Teach League.

title: The Uncaged Leader with Lucas Sherraden
author: Lucas Sherraden
description:
Success doesn’t always feel like freedom.

On The Uncaged Leader, leadership coach and speaker Lucas Sherraden helps entrepreneurs, executives, and high-achieving leaders uncover the hidden internal programming driving stress, burnout, conflict, reactivity, and disconnection — even when everything looks successful on the outside.

Most leadership advice teaches people how to perform. Very little teaches leaders how to become free.

Through powerful stories, leadership psychology, practical frameworks, interviews, and honest conversations, Lucas helps leaders recognize their autopilot patterns, increase self-awareness, replace judgment with curiosity, and lead with greater peace, clarity, and purpose.

This podcast is for leaders who:

  • Feel trapped in pressure, performance, or constant responsibility
  • Are burned out, emotionally exhausted, or internally misaligned
  • Want healthier relationships, stronger teams, and meaningful success
  • Desire conscious leadership instead of reactive leadership
  • Are ready to break free from the invisible cage of unconscious stories and survival patterns

This is not hustle culture, surface-level motivation, or empty inspiration.

This is leadership transformation rooted in awareness, emotional resilience, curiosity-driven leadership, and lasting internal change.

If you’ve built success but still feel heaviness, frustration, anxiety, or disconnection… this show is for you.

Because the greatest limitation in leadership is rarely external.

It’s the invisible cage we never learned to question.

title: Duffy Diaries
author: Travis Michael
description: Duffy Diaries is a lifestyle podcast recorded on and around the water, featuring real conversations about business, dating, travel, and everyday chaos. Hosted by Travis Michael, with guests, stories, and perspectives you won’t hear anywhere else

title: Carrington’s Call
author: Carrington Dantzler
description: One man’s walk. A brotherhood’s calling. This is where faith meets the fire of reality. I’m not perfect but I know and serve a God who is. Join me on my walk and who knows we can walk together

title: Light in the Rain
author: Light in the Rain
description:
Light in the Rain is a place for honest conversations about life after loss, finding faith in the hardest seasons, and discovering hope when everything feels broken.

Hosted by Brady and Dominique Meakin, this podcast shares the raw, unfiltered journey of losing their four-year-old son, Koleson, while learning how to navigate grief, strengthen their marriage, raise their family, and trust God through it all.

Each episode is a reminder that even in life’s darkest storms, there is still light to be found. We’ll talk about grief, faith, parenting, marriage, mental health, healing, and the moments that have changed us forever. We’ll also share the story behind Cared By Koleson, our nonprofit dedicated to supporting families facing medical challenges and unimaginable loss.

Whether you’re grieving, walking through a difficult season, supporting someone you love, or simply looking for encouragement, our prayer is that these conversations help you feel seen, understood, and reminded that you don’t have to walk through the rain alone.


Please feel free to reach out to us at litrain44@gmail.com or comment on our youtube channel! We would love to hear from you guys or answer any questions!

Because even when the skies are dark, God is still faithful and there’s always Light in the Rain.

title: Living Your Daily Life Vacation State of Being
author: Nicole Marino
description:
What if that feeling you get on vacation — light, unbothered, fully yourself, and drive to go after soul goals — wasn't something you had to wait a year for?

Hosted by Nicole Marino — keynote speaker, storyteller, entrepreneur, and founder of PlatformRevival and Bigshot Media — this show is about building a life that feels like that, every day. In 2009, Nicole moved from Boston to Napa Valley with two suitcases, no job, and an arrangement for a long-duration Napa hotel stay. What she found on the other side of that leap became the foundation for everything she teaches now: mindset shifts, nervous system regulation, the daily habits that turn a vacation feeling into a permanent one, and aligning with truest self.

Some episodes are Nicole solo, unpacking a framework, a mindset shift, or a piece of her own story. Others are conversations with guests — coaches, entrepreneurs, celebrities, and everyday people — about reinvention, resilience, and what it actually takes to build a life around what lights you up.

If you're craving more ease, more clarity, and less stress — and you're ready to stop waiting for permission to live the life your soul's been whispering about — this is your show.

title: Stand on Truth
author: Marco Caracas
description: Stand On Truth is a podcast dedicated to honest conversations about faith, truth, purpose, and what it means to live with conviction in today's world. Hosted by Marcos Caracas, each episode explores real-life experiences, biblical principles, personal growth, relationships, culture, and the challenges people face while pursuing a life grounded in truth. Whether you're a lifelong believer, new to faith, or simply searching for deeper meaning, Stand On Truth is a place for thoughtful discussions that encourage, challenge, and inspire.

title: Archivo Cero
author: PAF Studios
description:
Cada historia comienza con un expediente olvidado. Una inteligencia artificial que desafía a sus creadores, documentos capaces de reescribir la historia y decisiones que pueden cambiar el futuro. Archivo Cero es una serie de ficción sonora donde el suspenso, la tecnología, el misterio y los dilemas humanos se entrelazan en relatos inmersivos que te harán cuestionar qué es verdad y qué permanece oculto. Abre el archivo. Escucha la evidencia.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Who Did That Voice
author: Trenton Larkin
description: Who Did That Voice? takes you behind the scenes of entertainment, revealing the voices and minds behind your favorite characters, music, and commercials. From animation to blockbuster films like Transformers, I mostly solo-host, with occasional interviews. We explore voice actors, directors, coaches, and composers. If the name sounds familiar, you may know me from our 2016-2019 launch or brief 2021 revival. Now, we’re back for the 10th anniversary, celebrating with a new episode on the Fourth of July. Join me each Friday as we dive into the lives of those who worked with or Who Did That Voice!

title: The Blue Report!
author: Alex and Milo
description: All about The Dodgers and Baseball

title: Work the Room with Lauren Ansley
author: Lauren Ansley
description:
Most meetings don't need more information. They need more connection.
Work the Room is a short, practical podcast with speaker, emcee, and audience engagement expert Lauren Ansley.

In under 10 minutes, you'll discover actionable ideas to improve presentations, engage audiences, make networking more meaningful, help learning stick, and create meetings people actually remember. Because connection doesn't just happen.

You have to Work the Room.

title: Beyond The Stay: Live from Baha Mar
author: Baha Mar
description:
Join hosts Anastarcia Palacious and Renaldo Dorsett as they sit down with the people who shape Baha Mar: the chefs, the artists, the visionaries, and the guests to uncover the moments, the culture, and the quiet details that turn a destination into something unforgettable.

Because the real story of Baha Mar isn't in the brochure. It's in the people who live it!

title: A Bit Off
author: Dr. Rahav Dor
description: A Bit Off is a podcast about surprising ideas in science, technology, and engineering. We speak with researchers, engineers, and thinkers from around the world and translate serious ideas into clear, engaging conversations for curious listeners. From the systems that shape our daily lives to the discoveries that may shape the future, each episode looks at the world from a slightly different angle.

title: The Happy Choir
author: The Choir
description: Jubilate Deo! Sing joyfully to God with Tori, Jayleen, & Jorel - 3 expert, senior chant choir members - as they discuss a wide array of theological and spiritual topics. From big doctrinal discussions to simple soliloquies on everyday life, we pray you can find fruit in our talks!

title: JUSTICE AND BEYOND
author: Sargunpreet kaur
description: This is a podcast where we talk about law, leadership and What’s happening in the world in simple and easy way. In every episode, I’ll talk about a law topic and about an interesting news story and share a leadership lesson. My goal is to help people learn something new , think about different ideas,  and understanding the world little better.

title: Stephen 's Podcast
author: Stephen
description: Bible study for discernment of spirit mysteries hidden until today

title: Morse Code Practice With Stories!
author: MorseWords
description:
MorseWords creates Morse code reading and practice audio using public-domain literature, classic stories, and educational text.
Each episode is designed to help listeners practice recognizing Morse code patterns, improve reading fluency, and build familiarity with letters, words, and rhythm through longer-form listening practice.
