The content is intended for educational use, self-study, hobby learning, and anyone interested in Morse code, public-domain books, or audio-based practice.
https://www.morsewords.com/morse-code-books

title: Legislative Manners in Seeking Knowledge
author: Al-Kuttaab As-Salafy
description: Lessons on the importance of the Legislative Manners in seeking knowledge and beneficial details regarding this issue with evidence from the Book, the Sunnah, and narrations from the Scholars of Ħadīth.

title: RMB Think Summit 2026
author: RMB
description: The world is resetting in real time, reshaping markets, investment priorities and global influence. At the 2026 RMB Think Summit, economists, policymakers, strategists and global thinkers come together to unpack what that shift means for Africa now.

title: Franchise Spotlight with Katherine LeBlanc
author: Katherine LeBlanc
description:
Conversations with the leaders who are actually building franchising - from the executive suite to the field. Each season explores what success looks like right now, what franchise leaders should be paying attention to, and how the best brands are delivering real returns for their franchisees. Because at the end of the day, franchisee profitability isn't just a metric; it's the whole point.

Your host, Katherine LeBlanc, brings 13 years inside franchise systems, having led marketing across six brands spanning food, wellness, beauty, and experience. She's been in the room where these decisions get made. These conversations reflect that: direct, practitioner-level, and built for franchise leaders who don't have time for surface-level takes.

title: Menopause Chats - Real Women, Real Conversations
author: Erin Daruszka
description:
Erin Daruszka is a somatic & intuitive coach and a certified Menopause Coach who operates Pause and Align - helping GenX women in the "in-between." At: www.pauseandalign.com 


This podcast was created to share the stories of the every day perimenopausal and menopausal woman - think StoryCorps for menopause. It represents real stories from the perspective of eavesdropping on two friends chatting. These stories are not intended as medical advice, but for information and entertainment. Please seek a medical professional for specific advice regarding your menopause journey.

title: The Hearing Professional Podcast
author: ihspodcast
description: An original podcast from the International Hearing Society featuring conversations with the professionals, innovators, and thought leaders driving the future of hearing health care.

title: The All-In Texas Poker Podcast
author: All-In Texas Poker
description: The All-In Texas Poker Podcast is dedicated to delivering in-depth coverage of the Texas poker scene through news, analysis, and conversations. Hosted by Chet Chaudhari and Chris McCauley, each episode explores the stories shaping poker across the state — from major tournaments and card room developments to legislation, business, and updates on Texan players across the global circuit. Join us as we go all-in on Texas poker.

title: The Long View
author: The Kennan Institute
description: The Long View explores ideas, long-form arguments, and the space where public debate meets rigorous research. The series examines the former Soviet Union and the global policy questions it raises, bringing scholars and practitioners into conversation. Through interviews and discussions, The Long View connects academic insight with real-world relevance, continuing the Kennan Institute’s tradition of clear, accessible analysis.

title: The Silent Bullet: Professor Kennedy's Scientific Mysteries
description:
In a world teetering on the brink of scientific discovery and criminal intrigue, Professor Craig Kennedy emerges as a brilliant detective, solving mysteries with the keen eye of a scientist. "Silent Bullet" plunges listeners into a series of electrifying adventures where logic and innovation clash with the underbelly of crime. Alongside his intrepid reporter friend, Walter Jameson, Kennedy unravels puzzles that seem unsolvable, employing groundbreaking techniques that blend science with sleuthing.

Each tale offers a unique glimpse into early 20th-century life, where the boundaries of technology and morality blur. From a chilling murder shrouded in secrecy to a baffling case of theft involving cutting-edge inventions, Kennedy's journey reveals not just the facts but the human stories behind each crime.

Listeners will find themselves captivated by the intricate plots and the engaging dynamics between Kennedy and Jameson. As they navigate through twists and turns, they’ll discover how ingenuity can illuminate even the darkest corners of mystery. Prepare to be drawn into a world where every silent bullet carries the weight of a thrilling secret waiting to be uncovered. "Silent Bullet" is more than just a detective story; it's a celebration of intellect, friendship, and the relentless pursuit of truth.

title: Pass Focus: A Riftbound TCG Podcast
author: gyrosean
description: A podcast focused on discussing everything related to playing and collecting the Riftbound TCG, featuring gyrosean and rils2

title: Lady Molly of Scotland Yard: The Female Detective Pioneer
description: In 'Lady Molly of Scotland Yard,' discover the captivating tales of Molly Robertson-Kirk, one of the first female detectives in fiction. Written by Baroness Emma Orczy in 1910, these short stories showcase Molly's sharp intellect and keen observational skills as she solves crimes with finesse. Unlike traditional detectives of her time, Molly relies on her wits rather than physical strength, challenging the norms of her era. Each story unfolds a new mystery, revealing not only the complexities of crime but also the societal expectations of women. As you listen, you'll be drawn into a world where cleverness and cunning reign supreme. Orczy masterfully blends intrigue and insight, making these stories not just entertaining but also thought-provoking. Join Lady Molly on her adventures and witness how she navigates the challenges of being a woman in a man's world while outsmarting criminals and unraveling mysteries. This collection is a must-listen for fans of detective fiction, historical stories, and those who appreciate strong female characters forging their paths.

title: GeauxtimeSports with Nick
author: Nicholaus Taylor
description:
Geauxtimesports is an all-inclusive podcast featuring ALL LSU sports with a main focus in Football. Hosted with the passion of a true Tiger, Geauxtimesports hosted by Nicholaus Taylor dives deep into LSU football — breaking down recruiting, game strategy, player development, and the biggest moments shaping the program’s future. But the show doesn’t stop there. You’ll also get sharp, entertaining coverage of LSU baseball, basketball, gymnastics, softball, track, and every sport where the Tigers compete for glory.

Whether it’s insider insights, bold takes, interviews, or weekly rundowns, Geauxtimesports delivers everything LSU fans crave. If it’s happening in Baton Rouge, it’s happening here.

title: Going to the End
author: Gene Boyd
description: Might you be called to equip and prepare, both yourself and others, for the end of the last days? Do you ever feel like the words Jesus spoke to Peter, "Feed my sheep" also speak to your soul? Do you want to be strong and courageous? Would you rather follow Christ than men, so that you can be your best person to the praise, glory, and honor of our Saviour? Are you ready to walk in love, even if this means laying down your life? If you answer yes to some or all of these, then we have a lot in common. Let's get going...

title: 10 Tales From The Script V1
author: Mr.Andrew
description: A collection of 10 Screenplays, Pilots, Skits, Scenes, and more, performed by over 25 talented performers for your listening pleasure!

title: We're Fine...Aren't We?
author: Anne and Katie
description: Life as women in our 40s

title: TEDxBend
author: StoryBooth
description:
TEDxBend is a community relationship with a goal to strengthen our bonds with Central Oregon. With each idea that fuels our dreams and energizes us to take action, comes amazing opportunity for Central Oregon. This relationship speaks of being part of something meaningful, something authentic, something that has tangible value. The power of stories told transcends a single day—and creates energy to be inspired by and to act upon. That energy is TEDxBend.

Step into the conversation. Join us on the TEDxBend Podcast and participate in community conversations focused around the theme of the 2026 TEDxBend event, Rebirth—a shared invitation to shed what no longer serves us and step into what’s possible next. Before you hit play, take a look at your own horizon: If you could intentionally begin again or hit 'refresh' on one area of your life, career, or perspective today, what would it look like, and why?

Hear from event organizers, speakers and community members as they engage in purpose driven conversation brought to you by The StoryBooth Experience.

title: The Reluctant Adventurers Podcast
author: reluctantadventurers
description:
Welcome to The Reluctant Adventurers.

Brought together by chance, bound by circumstance, and rarely prepared for what comes next, our heroes never intended to become adventurers.

They don't seek glory. They don't chase danger. And most days, they'd rather be anywhere else.

Yet somehow, despite bad plans, questionable decisions, and more than a little luck, they keep managing to save the day.

Whether by bravery, friendship, or complete accident, another adventure always seems to find them.

These are the tales of The Reluctant Adventurers.

Now, let's join our unlikely heroes as their story continues.

Join us each week as we tell of their adventures.

title: 🇺🇸 English | SunnyVerse AI, Data & Tech Talks with Twinkle & Celestyn
author: SunnyVerse AI Labs
description:
Welcome to SunnyVerse AI, Data & Tech Talks with Twinkle & Celestyn from SunnyVerse AI Labs — where ideas become execution across AI, data, analytics, automation, cloud, and intelligent systems. 
Join Twinkle, SunnyVerse's Concept Clarity Intelligence, and Celestyn, SunnyVerse's Architectural Intelligence, as they explore Python, SQL, Power BI, machine learning, AI engineering, APIs, product thinking, renewable forecasting, and enterprise systems through Sunny's systems-thinking lens. Each episode connects concepts to practical workflows, modern technologies, and execution-ready solutions.

title: Tell Your Friends wit Myche
author: Myche
description:
Join me, Myche, a melanated woman, life coach, herbalist, and holistic nutritionist, as I kick off this transformative podcast dedicated to uplifting and empowering our community. I'm passionate about addressing the issues that impact us as melanated Americans, from systemic challenges like health disparities and economic barriers to the strengths and triumphs that make our community thrive.

     Throughout the episodes, I'll dive into practical life coaching tips and tools to help you navigate everyday struggles, build resilience, and achieve your goals. You'll also get a taste of my herbal expertise with simple, effective recipes and remedies- think soothing teas for stress relief or nutrient-packed meals to boost your energy. And because no conversation is complete without heart, I'll share personal stories from my own life, including how herbalism and holistic nutrition have been my anchors through tough times.

    Whether you're seeking inspiration, actionable advice, or a safe space to reflect, this podcast is for you. Tune in, get empowered, and let's make real changes together. Subscribe now and join the conversation- your story matters!

title: Dr. Eric Berg’s Podcast
author: Dr. Eric Berg
description: Dr. Eric Berg

title: Spiritual, biblical, and prayerful approaches to the mental health crisis
author: Rick Price
description: The mental health crisis is getting worse.  Is spirituality part of the solution to this awful crisis?  Let's explore.

title: Além da Maré - Podcast
author: Bianca Carla
description:
Em um mundo onde os oceanos sustentam vidas, economias e culturas, entender como usamos e cuidamos dos recursos pesqueiros nunca foi tão urgente.
O podcast Além da Maré nasce como um espaço de diálogo entre ciência, gestão e realidade. Aqui, redes não capturam apenas peixes, mas também ideias, debates e soluções. Com uma abordagem acessível e ao mesmo tempo profunda, este podcast conecta especialistas, experiências reais e reflexões necessárias para presente e construir o futuro dos nossos recursos aquáticos.

title: The Naughty Northern Gamer Podcast
author: Bear Wood
description:
Welcome to The Naughty Northern Gamer Podcast, where we explore the games that shaped generations.
From the hidden stories behind legendary franchises to retrospectives on gaming’s biggest successes and failures, each episode dives deep into the worlds, characters, mechanics, and moments that made gaming what it is today.
Whether it’s Assassin’s Creed, Battlefield, Call of Duty, Halo, Red Dead Redemption, or forgotten classics, this podcast celebrates gaming history one story at a time.
If you'd like what I do, you can also find me on YouTube and Patreon @ That Naughty Northern Gamer

title: The Rowdy Sports Show
author: Rowdy Sports
description:
The Rowdy Sports Show brings you conversations with the people shaping sports across Georgia and beyond. Produced by Rowdy Sports, an Atlanta-based digital sports media outlet, each episode features athletes, coaches, executives, broadcasters, and other newsmakers from the teams, leagues, and organizations we cover.


Why "Rowdy"? Because that's who we're named after—the passionate Southern sports fan. Loud. Energetic. Loyal. Enthusiastic. The kind of fan who lives every play and never misses a game.


If you love sports and the stories behind the games, you've found your home. Welcome to the Rowdy Sports Show. For even more local sports coverage, visit RowdySports.TV, follow us on social media, and subscribe to the Rowdy Roundup newsletter to stay connected with the latest news, interviews, and exclusive content. https://rowdysports-newsletter-44d5d2.beehiiv.com/

title: Steel Toes and Sippy Cups
author: Emz
description:
12-hour shifts. Mom life. Mental exhaustion. Real conversations.
Steel Toes & Sippy Cups is the podcast for the women doing it all — working in the plants, raising families, surviving burnout, and still showing up every day.
From chemical plant life to motherhood, relationships, chaos, healing, and hard truths, this is a space for raw stories, unfiltered conversations, and women who are stronger than they realize.
Because behind every hard hat is a real woman with a real story. 🎙️🩷

title: Invisible Battles
author: Jake
description: The Quiet Mind Files explores the hidden side of mental health through science, psychology, real-life patterns, and practical insights. Every episode uncovers the emotional habits, silent struggles, and powerful ways people build resilience. This podcast is designed to educate, inspire, and reduce the stigma around mental well-being while encouraging listeners to seek professional help whenever needed. Calm, thoughtful, and evidence-informed, it's a space where every mind matters.

title: Let's Talk: Life, Leadership and Making Disciples
author: MCNCR
description: Welcome to Let's Talk, a leadership podcast designed to train, inform and inspire church planters, pastors, staff members, volunteers and disciple-makers. Let's Talk is brought to you by the North Central Region of the Missionary Church, where we want to empower everyday people to love like Jesus and live like missionaries.

title: StormCast
author: Manchester Storm
description: The official podcast of Manchester Storm - the only place to hear from players, coaches and anyone connected with the Storm

title: AMIA Unpacked
author: amiapodcast
description: An original podcast series from the American Medical Informatics Association exploring the real-world impact of AI and innovation in healthcare through conversations with clinicians, researchers, health system leaders, and emerging voices across the informatics community.

title: THE COMFORT SHOW NOT YOUR FLAWS, OPEN WOUNDS AND SCARRED HEARTS
author: COMFORT
description:
Whether I feel like it or not...
Sad or happy... Anxious or moody... 
Joy or pain... Laughter or not...
The Comfort Show will go on.
This is how I express myself and help others live, learn, and grow. 
I might save a life one day.
Worrying is part of life. But the show whether i feel like it or not , sad or happy, anxious or moody, joy or pain The Comfort show will go on... it’s a promise. 
Worrying is part of life but THE SHOW MUST GO ON!!Because I speak my truth, my salvation, my testimony.
I’m more than my leg. Disability isn’t a flaw. 
Original. Authentic. God’s time 💛

title: The Financial Blueprint
author: Kyle Ryan, CFP
description:
Welcome to The Financial Blueprint with Kyle Ryan, CFP®—your go-to resource for a smarter, more personalized approach to money.
This channel is for every stage of the financial journey, from beginners to experienced DIY investors. We also have real conversations with industry professionals, and honest takes on trending social media financial advice.
Securities and advisory services offered through LPL Financial, a registered investment advisor, Member FINRA / SIPC
The LPL Financial registered representative(s) associated with this website may discuss and/or transact business only with resi

title: 5812 CHURCH
author: 5812 CHURCH COLLECTIVE
description:
"Experience messages, podcasts, and leadership equipping,.All in one place, from our 5812 Church Collective."


Mensajes inspiradores, podcasts y herramientas de liderazgo: todo en un solo lugar, de parte de nuestra comunidad 5812 Church Collective

title: The Poisoned Pen: Professor Kennedy's Scientific Mysteries
description: Dive into the thrilling world of Professor Craig Kennedy, a brilliant detective who uses science to crack cases. In 'Poisoned Pen,' Arthur B. Reeve invites you to explore twelve captivating mysteries that showcase Kennedy's keen intellect and innovative methods. Alongside his trusty friend, reporter Walter Jameson, Kennedy tackles perplexing crimes that challenge conventional thinking. Whether it's uncovering the truth behind a suspicious death or unraveling a tangled web of deceit, each story promises to keep you guessing until the very end. Reeve's engaging writing style and clever plots create a rich listening experience that will appeal to fans of classic detective fiction. Join Kennedy on his adventures and discover how science becomes the ultimate tool for solving the most baffling mysteries.

title: Kuriosity Saves
author: Sheena Keesha
description: Come, let’s reason together — not apart from God, but with him. This podcast is for seekers, believers, and the spiritually curious. Kuriosity Saves explores divine questions God asks throughout Scripture and the ones we carry in our own hearts. God’s questions aren’t informational; they’re invitational. Blending cinematic storytelling with biblical insight, this podcast reveals how holy curiosity surrendered to Christ can lead to encounter, restoration, and new life. The title cleverly integrates the Greek word Kurios, meaning ”Lord,” with a play on a famous idiom ”Curiosity killed the cat, but satisfaction brought it back”.

title: CIO Unplugged
author: Exponential-e
description: In this insightful episode, Zine Giles from Exponential-e interviews David Newey, Chief Digital Transformation Officer, covering crucial topics like public health, space innovation, and the latest technology trends benefiting humanity. They dive into sensitive discussions around the healthcare sector, exploring how digital transformation is shaping both public services and emerging innovations. Don't miss this fascinating conversation about the future of technology and its impact on society!

title: Read the Room with Kass and Alysha
author: Read the Room with Kass and Alysha
description:
Read the book. Read the room. 🎙️📖 
We read everything from fantasy and science fiction to literary fiction and romances giving you a vast range of recommendations to add to your never ending TBR!
Episodes out every Sunday ✨

title: Lightroom Testimonies
author: Milana B
description: The Lightroom Testimonies is a faith-centered talk series hosted by media personality Milana B, where real life meets real purpose. This isn’t just another faith podcast—each episode features honest conversations with industry shifters, creatives, and leaders navigating life in every season. Rooted in the belief that everyone carries a light, every testimony shared is meaningful, powerful, and worth telling. This space is designed to uplift, inspire, and remind you that your journey has purpose and long-lasting impact. Come as you are—leave reminded of who you’re called to be.

title: Ukrainian Mountaintop Podcast
author: romang4
description: The Ukrainian Mountaintop Podcast brings you the latest sermons, reflections, and community conversations from the Ukrainian Catholic community, located in the Catskill Mountains of New York State. Each episode features inspiring teachings from our parish clergy, discussions on Ukrainian spiritual traditions, and highlights of events and initiatives important to the broader Ukrainian Mountaintop community. Whether you’re near or far, this podcast keeps you connected to the life and spirit of our church and community.

title: KinyaneiTorah4Life
author: The Path4life - Nochum Malinowitz
description: Learn The 48 Kinyanei Torah in 4 Minutes!

title: Truth and Tech
author: Travis
description: A place to discuss truth and technology from a Christian perspective.

title: The Midnight Passenger: Secrets and Schemes
description: Randall Clayton finds himself trapped in a web of deceit and danger. After his father's death, he relies on Hugh Worthington, his father's former business partner, for guidance. But Worthington’s intentions are far from noble; he harbors dark secrets and a ruthless plan to exploit Clayton for his own gain. Meanwhile, Clayton's roommate, Arthur Ferris, has his sights set on Worthington's daughter, complicating matters further. As Clayton navigates his precarious job in New York, he realizes he is being watched and manipulated by those he thought he could trust. In 'Midnight Passenger,' join Clayton as he uncovers layers of betrayal, confronts his enemies, and fights to reclaim his life. This gripping detective fiction explores themes of loyalty, ambition, and the cost of betrayal, keeping listeners on the edge of their seats with every twist and turn.

title: AI Ads+ Podcast
description: The AI Ads+ Podcast covers how AI video ads on YouTube Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn and everywhere else are changing the world of commerce. AI is also being used for scripting, campaign planning, and ad targeting. With hosts Dane Golden and Tolis Dokianos.

title: The Just And The Unjust: Secrets and Betrayal
description: John North finds himself in a nightmare after being framed for murder. As he battles to prove his innocence, he discovers that the people closest to him may not be who they seem. Friends turn into suspects, and hidden secrets threaten to unravel everything he thought he knew. While the legal system should protect him, even those in law have their own agendas, complicating his quest for justice. The only person who knows the real killer's identity—a drunken man—has vanished without a trace. In this thrilling detective fiction, Vaughan Kester weaves a story filled with tension, unexpected turns, and moral dilemmas. Will John North uncover the truth before it's too late? Dive into a world where trust is a luxury and betrayal lurks around every corner.

title: Candid Conversations with Dr. Latonia
author: Latonia Lewis
description:
Welcome to Candid Conversations with Dr. Latonia, where we talk about faith, feelings, healing, grief, growth, and all the things that don’t always get discussed in church or in everyday life.
As a therapist, minister, author, and educator, I’ve learned that many people are carrying burdens, asking hard questions, and searching for hope. This podcast is a safe place for honest conversations about real life, emotional wellness, spiritual growth, and the challenges we all face along the way.
So grab your coffee, pull up a chair, and let’s have a candid conversation.

title: The Pagan Madonna: A Tale of Intrigue in Shanghai
description: In the bustling streets of Shanghai, secrets lurk around every corner. 'Pagan Madonna' by Harold MacGrath takes you on a thrilling journey through a world of crime, art, and deception. When Jean Norman, a dedicated Red Cross nurse from the United States, crosses paths with Anthony Cleigh, a wealthy art collector, and Ling Foo, a cunning pawn shop owner, a web of mystery unfolds over a seemingly innocuous string of beads. As their lives intertwine, each character grapples with trust, ambition, and the shadowy underbelly of art collecting. Discover how fate, often viewed as the 'Blind Madonna,' guides their choices, leading to unexpected twists and dangerous confrontations. This captivating detective fiction not only explores human motives but also delves into the allure of art and the risks taken to possess it. Join the intrigue and uncover the truth behind the Pagan Madonna.

title: Kano Mixes
author: Kutlwano Monyae
description:
My people, welcome to the land of good music.
We are not bound by trends or what's hot here, we just play the music and let you listen and be blessed by it!
It is an honor to grace you with these mixes.
Thank you for your support!

title: Welcome to goodChatBot Podcast!
author: goodChatBot – AI Support
description:
goodChatBot, developed by Automatikly, provides AI chatbot software for Shopify ecommerce stores with automated support, multilingual chat, and conversion optimization tools. Listen to our podcast episodes to learn about AI automation, customer engagement, and Shopify optimization.

For more details, visit https://goodchatbot.com

title: The Rooted Method: Summer 2026
author: Julia Lynch
description: Private podcast for participants of the Rooted Method

title: Defining Freedom: An American Revolution Special
description:
America is obsessed with freedom. And a good story. Since the United States founding, we’ve enjoyed the narrative that America has always stood as a bastion of liberty. It’s a good story...if it were entirely true. In this audio special from WMHT Public Media and PBS, we explore the hidden history behind America’s quest for freedom. Produced in companion to KEN BURNS’S THE AMERICAN REVOLUTION.
Corporate funding for THE AMERICAN REVOLUTION was provided by Bank of America. Major funding was provided by The Better Angels Society and its members Jeannie and Jonathan Lavine with the Crimson Lion Foundation; and the Blavatnik Family Foundation. Major funding was also provided by David M. Rubenstein; The Robert D. and Patricia E. Kern Family Foundation; Lilly Endowment Inc.; and the following Better Angels Society members: Eric and Wendy Schmidt; Stephen A. Schwarzman; and Kenneth C. Griffin with Griffin Catalyst. Additional support for THE AMERICAN REVOLUTION was provided by: The Arthur Vining Davis Foundations; The Pew Charitable Trusts; Gilbert S. Omenn and Martha A. Darling; Park Foundation; and the following Better Angels Society members: Gilchrist and Amy Berg; Perry and Donna Golkin; The Michelson Foundation; Jacqueline B. Mars; Kissick Family Foundation; Diane and Hal Brierley; John H. N. Fisher and Jennifer Caldwell; John and Catherine Debs; The Fullerton Family Charitable Fund; Philip I. Kent; Gail Elden; Deborah and Jon Dawson; David and Susan Kreisman; The McCloskey Family Charitable Trust; Becky and Jim Morgan; Carol and Ned Spieker; Mark A. Tracy; and Paul and Shelley Whyte. THE AMERICAN REVOLUTION was made possible, in part, with support from the Corporation for Public Broadcasting. Promotional material for Defining Freedom includes footage from Field Trip Visits the American Revolution and The Library of Congress.
MUSIC CREDIT
From The Four Seasons by Antonio Vivaldi
Live, Unedited performance
Wiedemann Recital Hall, Wichita State University
John Harrison – Violin | Robert Turizziani – Conductor
The Wichita State University Chamber Players
johnharrison.cc

title: AFTER SILENCE
author: Ciruntefla
description:
Real stories. Real moments. No motivation. Just life as it is.
Hosted by Tefla, a Yoruba boy telling stories about love, friendship, university life, mistakes, growth, Leo, Eilish, and the moments that stay with us long after they happen.
Welcome to After Silence.

title: Raunchmanse She Wrote: You'll Never Work in This Town Again
author: Amy strauss
description:
You'll Never Work in This Town Again is a limited podcast series by Silda Collins exploring the story behind the novel of the same name.


Each episode begins with an exclusive reading from the book before diving into the characters, their astrology, archetypes, and relationships, along with the real-life experiences that inspired the story. Part author commentary, part creative workshop, and part exploration of ambition, desire, workplace power, and transformation, this series reveals how fiction can illuminate the lives we actually live.


Whether you've already read the novel or are discovering it for the first time, you'll find behind-the-scenes insights, character analysis, astrological deep dives, and the creative journey behind one woman's descent into—and eventual escape from—the stories she believed about success.


The novel releases September 4, 2026. Preorder now: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0GX32ZFR2

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Sefer Ground
author: Sefer Ground
description: Safeguarding Jewish and Christian Identity

title: The We Is Smart Podcast
author: Lucky Dub Network
description:
Welcome to The We Is Smart Podcast — a show designed for adults 40+ who still think, laugh, debate, and live out loud.
Each episode dives into meaningful (and sometimes hilarious) conversations about life, relationships, culture, trending topics, personal growth, and everything in between. We bring experience, perspective, and authenticity to the mic — because grown conversations just hit different.
Whether you're driving, working, relaxing, or just need a smart escape — this podcast is for you.

title: The Secrets of the Night: A Joseph Rouletabille Mystery
description: In 'Secret of the Night,' Gaston Leroux invites listeners into a thrilling world of mystery and intrigue through the eyes of Joseph Rouletabille, a young journalist turned detective. Set against a backdrop of suspense and unexpected twists, Rouletabille relies on sharp deductive reasoning to unravel complex cases, reminiscent of the legendary Sherlock Holmes. As he navigates the shadows of the night, Rouletabille faces challenges that test his intellect and bravery. With each clue he uncovers, the stakes grow higher, revealing dark secrets that could change everything. This captivating audio experience not only showcases Leroux's masterful storytelling but also delves into the human psyche, exploring themes of justice, truth, and the quest for understanding in a chaotic world. Join Rouletabille as he races against time to solve a mystery that will keep you on the edge of your seat. Perfect for fans of classic detective fiction and those who enjoy a good puzzle, 'Secret of the Night' promises to engage your mind and ignite your imagination.

title: Recovery Catalyst
description:
Recovery Catalyst Podcast hosted by Cat York, is dedicated to creating a supportive, honest space for candid discussions on mental health, addiction, recovery, healing, and breaking generational cycles. Each week, we dive into the messy, complex, and profound truth of finding a new, healthier legacy, sharing raw, authentic stories of resilience and reinvention. This is where a community connects, heals, and learns what it means to truly redefine their story, one authentic conversation at a time.


To Follow, Support or be a guest click https://linktr.ee/RecoveryCatalystPodcast

title: Your Psychic Powers and How to Develop Them
description: Instructions in how to develop your psychic powers including telepathy, clairvoyance, self-projection, reincarnation, and other topics. Seriously. "It must be distinctly understood … that I believe the vast bulk of the material presented in this book to be sound and helpful; the practical instructions are good, and the reader cannot go far wrong in following them. May he develop his own psychic powers, and gain light and understanding thereby!" (From Author’s Preface) (Summary by Todd)

title: Your Invisible Power
description: Genevieve Behrend was a teacher of Mental Science, a New Thought discipline created by Thomas Troward (1847- 1916). Your Invisible Power, published in 1921, is her first and most famous book. It is a guide to the use of visualization and other mental processes in life enhancement and the achievement of personal goals. (Summary by Algy Pug)

title: Wherein?
description: These studies in the book of Malachi were delivered as addresses to the students at Mr. Moody’s Bible School in Chicago, and then to my own congregation. They have also appeared in “The Record of Christian Work” in the United States, and in “Out and Out” in England. They are now sent out in a more permanent form, after careful revision, with the prayer that they may be used of God in calling His own children into the place of power without which form is nothing. (Introduction by G. Campbell Morgan)

title: Way of Peace
description: The Way of Peace is your guide to the power of meditation; self and truth; the acquirement of spiritual power; the realization of selfless love; entering into the infinite; saints, sages, and saviors; the law of service; and the realization of perfect peace. (Summary by Andrea Fiore)

title: Untroubled Mind
description: A very wise physician has said that “every illness has two parts—what it is, and what the patient thinks about it.” What the patient thinks about it is often more important and more troublesome than the real disease. What the patient thinks of life, what life means to him is also of great importance and may be the bar that shuts out all real health and happiness. The following pages are devoted to certain ideals of life which I would like to give to my patients, the long-time patients who have especially fallen to my lot. (Summary by Herbert J Hall - from the Preface)

title: Strength and How to Obtain It
description: In writing this book I have taken it as a commonplace that everyone—man, woman, and child—wants to be strong. Without strength—and by strength I mean health, vitality, and a general sense of physical well-being—life is but a gloomy business. Wealth, talent, ambition, the love and affection of friends, the pleasure derived from doing good to those about one, all these things may afford some consolation for being deprived of life’s chief blessing, but they can never make up for it. “But,” I am constantly being asked, “it is all very well for you to say this, and everyone of sense agrees with you; the point is, can we obtain this much-prized blessing?” In the vast majority of cases I can say unhesitatingly “Yes.” You can all be strong, all enjoy the heritage which was intended for you. - Summary by Eugen Sandow

title: Shining Gateway
description: Students of the works of James Allen all over the world will welcome with joy another book from his able pen. In this work we find the Prophet of Meditation in one of his deepest and yet most lucid expositions. How wonderfully he deals with fundamental principles! Here the reader will find no vague statement of generalities, for the writer enters with tender reverence into every detail of human experience. It is as though he came back to The Shining Gateway, and, standing there, he reviewed all the way up which his own feet have traveled, passing over no temptation that is common to man; knowing that the obstacles that barred his ascending pathway, or the clouds that at times obscured his vision, are the common experiences of all those who have set their faces towards the heights of Blessed Vision. As we read his words now, he seems to stand and beckon to us, saying, "Come on, my fellow Pilgrims; it is straight ahead to the Shining Gateway; I have blazed the track for you." In sending forth this, another posthumous volume from his pen, we have no doubt but that it will help many and many an aspiring soul up to the heights, until at last they too stand within The Shining Gateway. - Summary by Editor, The Shining Gateway

title: Life Learn Lesson With Aiman
author: Aiman
description:
🎙️ Life Learn Lesson With Aiman

Welcome to Life Learn Lesson With Aiman—a podcast where real-life experiences become powerful lessons for personal growth, success, and positive change.

Every episode explores inspiring stories, practical life lessons, leadership insights, career development, self-improvement, and the mindset needed to overcome challenges and create a meaningful life. Whether you're a student, a young professional, an entrepreneur, or someone striving to become the best version of yourself, this podcast is designed to motivate, educate, and empower you.

Join Aiman as we discuss resilience, purpose, productivity, finance, leadership, sustainability, and the everyday experiences that shape who we become. Together, we'll discover that every challenge carries a lesson, every setback creates an opportunity, and every day is a chance to grow.

New episodes every week.

🎧 Learn from life. Grow with purpose. Lead with impact.

title: Quit Your Worrying!
description: "People will worry, they do worry. What they want to know and need to learn is how to quit worrying. This I have attempted herein to show, with the full knowledge, however, that no one person's recipe can infallibly be used by any other person—so that, in reality, all I have tried to do is to set forth the means I have followed to teach myself the delightful lesson of serenity, of freedom from worry, and thereby to suggest to receptive minds a way by which they may possibly attain the same desirable end." - Summary by George Wharton James

title: Path of Prosperity
description: Summary from The Path of Prosperity: I looked around upon the world, and saw that it was shadowed by sorrow and scorched by the fierce fires of suffering. And I looked for the cause. I looked around, but could not find it; I looked in books, but could not find it; I looked within, and found there both the cause and the self-made nature of that cause. I looked again, and deeper, and found the remedy. I found one Law, the Law of Love; one Life, the Life of adjustment to that Law; one Truth, the truth of a conquered mind and a quiet and obedient heart. And I dreamed of writing a book which should help men and women, whether rich or poor, learned or unlearned, worldly or unworldly, to find within themselves the source of all success, all happiness, all accomplishment, all truth. And the dream remained with me, and at last became substantial; and now I send it forth into the world on its mission of healing and blessedness, knowing that it cannot fail to reach the homes and hearts of those who are waiting and ready to receive it.

title: Mystery of Pain
description: This book is addressed to the sorrowful, ... to whom their own or others' pain is a daily burden, upon whose hearts it weighs with an intolerable anguish. I seek to speak to these ; not as a teacher, but as a fellow. Sharing their feeling, and knowing well how vain is the attempt to throw off misery, or to persuade ourselves that life is better than it is, I would fain share with them also some thoughts that have seemed to me capable of casting a bright gleam of light athwart the darkness, and, if they are true, of bringing an immense, an incredible joy out of the very bosom of distress. - Summary by James Hinton

title: Mastery of Destiny
description: James Allen's inspirational and thought-provoking books have inspired millions. In The Mastery of Destiny, he instructs readers on developing self-control, willpower, concentration, and motivation. Through discipline and mindfulness, we can create a life of unending happiness, prosperity, and, most importantly, self-mastery. (Summary by Andrea Fiore)

title: Man: King of Mind, Body, and Circumstance
description: The problem of life consists in learning how to live. It is like the problem of addition or subtraction to the schoolboy. When mastered, all difficulty disappears, and the problem has vanished. All the problems of life, whether they be social, political, or religious, subsist in ignorance and wrong-living. As they are solved in the heart of each individual, they will be solved in the mass of men. Humanity at present is in the painful stage of “learning.” It is confronted with the difficulties of its own ignorance. As men learn to live rightly, learn to direct their forces and use their functions and faculties by the light of wisdom, the sum of life will be correctly done, and its mastery will put an end to all the “problems of evil.” To the wise, all such problems have ceased. Summary by James Allen.

title: Letters to a Daughter and A Little Sermon to School Girls
description: Helen Ekin Starrett, journalist, mother of two daughters, grandmother of seven granddaughters and teacher to many young girls at the Starrett School for Girls offers lessons in life and religion to girls about to "pass out from the guardianship of home into life with its duties and trials". (Summary by Bianca Kramer, quote from Letter I of this book)

title: Light on Life’s Difficulties
description: When a man enters a dark room he is not sure of his movements, he cannot see objects around him, or properly locate them, and is liable to hurt himself by coming into sudden contact with them. But let a light be introduced, and immediately all confusion disappears. Every object is seen, and there is no danger of being hurt. To the majority, life is such a dark room, and their frequent hurts—their disappointments, perplexities, sorrows and pains—are caused by sudden contact with principles which they do not see, and are therefore not prepared to deal with. But when the light of wisdom is introduced into the darkened understanding, confusion vanishes, difficulties are dissolved, all things are seen in their true place and proportion, and henceforth the man walks open-eyed and unhurt, in the clear light of wise comprehension. - Summary by James Allen

title: Ladies' Book of Etiquette, and Manual of Politeness
description: A guide for ladies, written in 1860, on what is accepted as correct behavior in polite society. The advice covers dress, travelling, staying in hotels, attending and giving parties and balls, making and receiving morning calls, letter writing, how to deal with servants, what accomplishments every well bred lady should be expected to acquire and how to choose a suitable husband. (Summary by Christine Lamberton)

title: How to Listen to Music
description: This book is "not written for professional musicians, but for untaught lovers of the art". It gives broad instruction on composers, styles, instruments, venues - and when to believe the critics. (Summary by Sarah Jennings)

title: God of All Comfort
description: This book is written to Christians who profess to believe the Bible as God's revelation, but whose "lives are filled with discomfort and unrest." Smith aims to show that the Bible's claim that God is the "the God of all comfort" is not an over-advertisement or misunderstanding, but that it is possible to avail ourselves of the doubts and heavy anxieties that plague so many Christians. By explaining God's part and man's part, Smith aims to show her readers that it is possible to overcome feelings of defeat and despair and find rest and peace in Christ. Abundant life and genuine enduring joy are available to all believers - even you. Deep and lasting peace, beyond all Earthly circumstances, is possible. (Introduction by Rachel Craig)

title: Gerrard Street Mystery and Other Weird Tales
description: John Charles Dent, the author of the following remarkable stories, was born in Kendal, Westmorland, England, in 1841. His parents emigrated to Canada shortly after that event, bringing with them, of course, the youth who was afterwards to become the Canadian author and historian. Mr. Dent received his primary education in Canadian schools, and afterwards studied law, becoming in due course a member of the Upper Canada Bar. He only practised for a few years, then returned to England to pursue a literary career, writing mostly for periodicals. After remaining in England for several years, Mr. Dent and his family moved to Boston, in America, for about two years. But he finally returned to Canada, accepting a journalistic position in Toronto. Mr. Dent proceeded to write 'The Canadian Portrait Gallery', which ran to four large volumes, 'The Last Forty Years: Canada since the Union of 1841', and a 'History of the Rebellion in Upper Canada'.This collected work of his short fiction, contributed by their author at considerable intervals to different periodicals, was published posthumously. The stories themselves are delightfully anchored in the physical geography of Upper Canada (or in the case of 'Gagtooth's Image', Illinois), and have a mystical and spooky air about them. (Summary by Pipesdreams)

title: Game of Life and How to Play It
description: Florence Scovel Shinn, an illustrator living in New York City, became a teacher of New Thought after a divorce. New Thought was a movement which holds the belief that individuals can create their own reality through intentional thoughts and prayer, much like the current Law of Attraction movement. The Game of Life and How to Play It is her first book, and is remarkable for being written by a woman and meant for a genteel female audience. (Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Eight Pillars of Prosperity (Version 2)
description: “Prosperity, like a house, is a roof over a man’s head, affording him protection and comfort. A roof presupposes a support, and a support necessitates a foundation. The roof of prosperity, then, is supported by the following eight pillars which are cemented in a foundation of moral consistency: 1. Energy 2. Economy 3. Integrity 4. System 5. Sympathy 6. Sincerity 7. Impartiality 8. Self-reliance A business built up on the faultless practice of all these principles would be so firm and enduring as to be invincible.” - Summary by James Allen

title: Creative Mind
description: This little book is an attempt to explain what each soul must discover for himself, that he stands in the midst of an eternal creative power which presses itself around his own thought, and casts back to him glorified all that he thinks. If it awakens within the consciousness of one single individual the realization that the mind of the Universal (which is the only mind that there is) is his own mind that the creative power of this mind is his also; that the manifestation of this mind is his own individuality; that the love and power and peace of this mind is within himself, it will not be written in vain. May it then do much in simplifying and bringing to light some of the deeper mysteries and meanings of life. (Ernest Shurtleff Holmes)

title: College Freshman's Don't Book
description: A short, humorous guide of what not to do in your first year of college. (Summary by Jill Engle)

title: Christian's Secret of a Happy Life
description: This Christian book is written in a easy and understandable way, where the strong biblical points are clear and and applicable. The richness of the truths explained, will make one want to listen to it over and over again. The book writes about our part and God's part in the Christian life. It also reveal many secrets to the Christian life. "Today it is your happy privilege to prove, as never before, your loyal confidence in the Lord by starting out with Him on a life and walk of faith, lived moment by moment in absolute and childlike trust in Him." quote from the book. It is a must listen to Christian classic. (Summary by peppersnoodles93)

title: Chesterton Calendar
description: Go through the year, day by day, with the wit and wisdom of G.K. Chesterton! Compiled from the writings of 'G.K.C', both in verse and in prose, each day of the year is provided with a generally short quotation from one of his many works. Also includes a section apart for the moveable feasts. (Summary by Maria Therese)

title: Brief Diversions
description: A miscellany of what the author describes as "Tales, Travesties and Epigrams" all of them very short. They were written while Priestley was serving in the British Army during the First World War and before he was to establish himself as a novelist. (Summary by Anthony Ogus)

title: As a Man Thinketh
description: Allen's books illustrate the use of the power of thought to increase personal capabilities. Although he never achieved great fame or wealth, his works continue to influence people around the world, including the New Thought movement. Allen's most famous book, As a Man Thinketh, was published in 1902. It is now considered a classic self-help book. Its underlying premise is that noble thoughts make a noble person, while lowly thoughts make a miserable person. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: All These Things Added
description: In seeking for pleasures here and rewards hereafter men have destroyed (in their hearts) the Temple of Righteousness, and have wandered from the Kingdom of Heaven. By ceasing to seek for earthly pleasures and heavenly rewards, the Temple of Righteousness is restored and the Kingdom of Heaven is found. This truth is for those who are ready to receive it; and this book also is for those whose souls have been prepared for the acceptance of its teaching. (James Allen)

title: Above Life's Turmoil
description: The turmoil of the world we cannot avoid, but the disturbances of mind we can overcome. The duties and difficulties of life claim our attention, but we can rise above all anxiety concerning them. Surrounded by noise, we can yet have a quiet mind; involved in responsibilities, the heart can be at rest; in the midst of strife, we can know the abiding peace. The twenty pieces which comprise this book, unrelated as some of them are in the letter, will be found to be harmonious in the spirit, in that they point the reader towards those heights of self-knowledge and self-conquest which, rising above the turbulence of the world, lift their peaks where the Heavenly Silence reigns. (Summary from the book)

title: The Green Rust: A Detective's Quest for Truth
description: When a millionaire falls gravely ill, he discovers a shocking truth: his life is in danger. With his time running out, he turns to his attorney for one last favor—find his estranged niece, Oliva Cresswell, and keep her safe. As Oliva, a cashier in a bustling West End store, navigates her ordinary life, she has no idea she is the key to a fortune and the target of a sinister plot. Just as the pieces start to come together, tragedy strikes: the millionaire is brutally murdered in his sickbed. Now, Oliva must unravel the mystery of her uncle's death and uncover the hidden threats surrounding her. With twists and turns at every corner, Edgar Wallace crafts a gripping tale of suspense, inheritance, and danger. Join Oliva in her quest for truth and survival as she faces foes both known and unknown. Will she discover the identity of the murderer before it's too late? This detective fiction masterpiece will keep you on the edge of your seat until the very last moment.

title: True Crime Podcast with Nick and Katie
author: More episodes available on YouTube
description:
Nick and Katie | Every Friday | True Crime Podcast
Nick and Katie are back for another episode of the True Crime Podcast. Travelling back in time to uncover the bizarre and shocking truth of murder cases forgotten to time.
#TrueCrime #Podcast

title: Our Old Home
description: These essays, based on Hawthorne’s stay in England from 1853 to 1857 as American Consul in Liverpool, were first published in the form of a series of travel articles for The Atlantic Monthly. In these writings, he displays his humor, his empathetic nature, his pride in his country, and sometimes his sharp judgment of others. He shares with us the difficulties of being a consul in the 1850’s, takes us on a tour with him through rural England and Scotland, shows us the splendors of London, and the horrors of the poverty that so many suffered. (Introduction by Margaret)

title: Menopause - It was this all along
author: Roshini Sanah Jaiswal
description: You're not the first to feel it, you're not going crazy. It was Menopause all along!

title: Red Men And White
description: These eight stories are made from our Western Frontier as it was in a past as near as yesterday and almost as by-gone as the Revolution; so swiftly do we proceed. They belong to each other in a kinship of life and manners, and a little through the nearer tie of having here and there a character in common. Thus they resemble faintly the separate parts of a whole, and gain, perhaps, something of the invaluable weight of length; and they have been received by my closest friends with suspicion. ...When our national life, our own soil, is so rich in adventures to record, what need is there for one to call upon his invention save to draw, if he can, characters who shall fit these strange and dramatic scenes? One cannot improve upon such realities. If this fiction is at all faithful to the truth from which it springs, let the thanks be given to the patience and boundless hospitality of the Army friends and other friends across the Missouri who have housed my body and instructed my mind. And if the stories entertain the ignorant without grieving the judicious I am content. (Summary from Author's Preface)

title: Strictly Business: More Stories of the Four Million
description: O. Henry is the pen name of American writer William Sydney Porter (1862-1910). His short stories are known for their wit, wordplay, characterization and the clever use of twist endings. Indeed, O. Henry is often credited with defining the short story as a literary art form. Strictly Business: More Stories of the Four Million, is a collection of twenty-three short stories that was first published in 1908. The setting is New York City in the first decade of the 1900’s (the Four Million in the subtitle is a reference to the population of the city at that time as well as to the title of an earlier collection of his short stories). You will meet a wide variety of characters from all walks of life who are trying to make a living via honest (and sometimes dishonest) means. Personal interactions and business transactions abound, but sometimes there is a twist. But don’t take it personally if you get fooled, after all it is Strictly Business. - Summary by Warren Kati

title: Heart of the West
description: A collection of short stories by the legendary O. Henry. (Summary by sidhu177)

title: Dragon Talk
author: Dragon Podcasts
description: Produced and hosted by Dragon Podcast Manager, Ryan Record, Dragon Talk is a student-focused podcast highlighting topics of interest to our diverse student population at Howard Community College. Tune in to find out what’s happening in Dragon Country!

title: Man who Understood Women, and Other Stories
description: This is a collection of 20 short stories by English novelist and playwright Leonard Merrick. The stories concern varied topics, and succeed to evoke a charming picture of England in the early 20th century. It is surprising that Merrick's work is no longer known to the public, as he was one of the most highly regarded writers of his own time. William Dean Howells wrote of him, for instance, "I can think of no recent fictionist of his nation who can quite match with Mr. Merrick in that excellence [of "shapeliness" or form in the novel]. This will seem great praise, possibly too great, to the few who have a sense of such excellence; but it will probably be without real meaning to most, though our public might well enjoy form if it could once be made to imagine it." (quoted in wikipedia). - Summary by Carolin

title: Edgewater People
description: A collection of interrelated short stories concerning the townfolk of a few small New England villages at the turn of the last century. (Summary by BellonaTimes)

title: Lucy Maud Montgomery Short Stories, 1909-1922
description: L.M. Montgomery was a Canadian author, best known for a series of novels that began with Anne of Green Gables, published in 1908. Once published, Anne of Green Gables was an immediate success. The central character, Anne, an orphaned girl, made Montgomery famous in her lifetime and gave her an international following. The first novel was followed by a series of sequels with Anne as the central character. Montgomery went on to publish 20 novels as well as 500 short stories and poems. Because many of the novels were set on Prince Edward Island in Canada, Canada and the Canadian province became literary landmarks. (Introduction by Wikipedia) Lucy Maud Montgomery Short Stories, 1896 to 1901Lucy Maud Montgomery Short Stories, 1902 to 1903Lucy Maud Montgomery Short Stories, 1904Lucy Maud Montgomery Short Stories, 1905 to 1906Lucy Maud Montgomery Short Stories, 1907 to 1908Lucy Maud Montgomery Short Stories, 1909 to 1922

title: New Year's Tangles and Other Stories
description: This is a collection of 40 short (some VERY short) stories from Christian author Pansy. They seem to have been taken from her magazine for children, The Pansy (1873–1896). Some are pure fiction; some are historical fiction, and some are true accounts of historical events. (Summary by TriciaG)

title: In Old Plantation Days
description: With this collection of short stories, Dunbar sought to draw on the success of his dialect poems by recreating and portraying the southern plantation during slavery. The stories focus on the stereotypical portrait of slaves as obedient workers happy to spend their lives in service of their benevolent owner. His attempt to find success was only partially realized, as his stories drew not only criticism but, in some cases, anger at their very stereotypical nature. The book itself, however, proved to be more lucrative than previous fiction works had been for the author. (Summary by Special Collections and Archives, Wright State University)

title: Astounding Stories 10, October 1930
description: Issue no. 10 of the magazine brings you:- Stolen Brains by Captain S.P. MeekThe Invisible Death by Victor Rousseau Prisoners on the Electron by Robert H. Leitfred Part 2 of Jetta of the Lowlands by Ray Cummings An Extra Man by Jackson Gee along with the Readers' Corner and interesting scientific facts (Summary by Annise)

title: Sunshine Factory
description: Seven very short sweet stories by Pansy that you will not soon forget! They are stories children will love, and everyone can enjoy. They will make you smile and laugh and bring tears to your eyes. And each one teaches an important lesson in a sweet, encouraging way. - Summary by fiddlesticks

title: Gallegher and other Stories
description: Set in 1890's USA, teen detective Gallegher is a copy boy for a local newspaper, but due to his superior sleuthing and feisty Huck Finn attitude, he ends up solving a serious crime. Walt Disney thought enough of the spunky Gallegher story to make a TV series of it back in 1965. Of course, Gallegher is only one of the 9 other interesting stories included, all period pieces, with interesting characters, all interestingly told. ( Summary by Michele Fry)

title: Legends of Vikramaditya
description: A collection of fascinating tales of adventure, bravery, wisdom, and generosity of the King Vikramaditya - Summary by Vineymala

title: Mashi and Other Stories
description: A 1918 collection of short stories, by the popular Bengali writer. (Summary by BellonaTimes)

title: Options
description: O. Henry needs no introduction of course; the man who made the short story with the surprise ending famous. These 16 stories are all wonderful examples of his word sculpting art. They include: "The Rose of Dixie"; The Third Ingredient; The Hiding of Black Bill; Schools and Schools; Thimble, Thimble; Supply and Demand; Buried Treasure; To Him Who Waits; He Also Serves; The Moment of Victory; The Head-Hunter; No Story; The Higher Pragmatism; Best-Seller; Rus in Urbe; A Poor Rule - Summary by Phil chenevert

title: Heptameron of the Tales of Margaret, Queen of Navarre, Volume 1
description: THE HEPTAMERON, first published posthumously in 1558, is divided into seven complete days containing 10 stories each, and an eighth day containing only 2 stories. The stories, many of which deal with love and infidelity, resulted in "accusations of looseness" by critics of the day. The author, Margaret of Navarre (also known as Margaret of Angoulême) became an influential woman in the intellectual and cultural circles of the French Renaissance. From an 1892 essay by the translator George Saintsbury: "In so large a number of stories with so great a variety of subjects, it naturally cannot but be the case that there is a considerable diversity of tone. But that peculiarity at which we have glanced more than once, the combination of voluptuous passion with passionate regret and a mystical devotion, is seldom absent for long together...The question, What is the special virtue of the Heptameron? I have myself little hesitation in answering. There is no book, in prose and of so early a date, which shows to me the characteristic of the time as it influenced the two great literary nations of Europe so distinctly as this book of Margaret of Angoulême… It combines a profound and certainly sincere—almost severe—religiosity with a very vigorous practice of some things which the religion it professes does not at all countenance. It has an almost morbidly pronounced simultaneous sense of the joys and the sorrows of human life, the enjoyment of the joys being perfectly frank, and the feeling of the sorrows not in the least sentimental. It unites a great general refinement of thought, manners, opinion, with an almost astonishing occasional coarseness of opinion, manners, thought…There, in a few words, is the secret of THE HEPTAMERON: the fear of God, the sense of death, the voluptuous longing and voluptuous regret for the good things of life and love that pass away." (Summary adapted from Wikipedia and excerpted from the text by lubee930)

title: Happy Prince and Other Tales
description: Collection of children’s stories written in 1888, dealing primarily with love and selfishness. These stories are generally sad, with a moralistic message. The collection includes: The Happy Prince, The Nightingale and the Rose, The Selfish Giant, The Devoted Friend, and The Remarkable Rocket. (Summary written by Joy Chan)

title: Cynical Miss Catherwaight
description: This is the story of Miss Catherwaight, collector of "dishonored honors" - medals of honor pawned by the persons they were awarded to. Part of Miss Catherwaight's collection are also the stories behind each award, and she tends to look down on their former owners for giving them away - until she finds a particular token in the shape of a heart... - Summary by Carolin

title: Gentle Grafter
description: If Jefferson "Parleyvoo" Pickens had appeared in print just a few years later, he might have been the "Gentle Grifter" instead of the "Gentle Grafter", the name O. Henry picked for him. His situation as an ethical graft artist gives Jeff an extra impediment in pursuing his craft, but he never wanted it to be too easy. The result is fourteen delightful tales for us and a number of new partners for him. With those partners (he always has at least one) he works his way through a number of confidence games. Some they win, some they lose, some go into extra innings. They seem never to end just the way you figure they will. In the end he conquers almost all, except for the English language, which often seems to defeat him. (Intro by Leslie Walden)

title: Little Minnie, and Other Stories
description: This is a little book of very short stories or vignettes, about children and intended for children. Many of the stories reference the included illustrations. Section 4, "Chinese", is a piece about how different the Chinese are from Americans. It is outdated and seems to put the Chinese in a negative light, which seems out of character for Pansy. (Summary by TriciaG)

title: My Buried Treasure
description: "This is a true story of a search for buried treasure. The only part that is not true is the name of the man with whom I searched for the treasure. Unless I keep his name out of it he will not let me write the story, and, as it was his expedition and as my share of the treasure is only what I can make by writing the story, I must write as he dictates. I think the story should be told, because our experience was unique, and might be of benefit to others. And, besides, I need the money." (From the text)

title: Astounding Stories 06, June 1930
description: Issue six of this seminal science-fiction magazine concludes the Ray Cummings story "Brigands of the Moon", and continues Murray Leinster's "Murder Madness". In addition there are three short stories, by various authors, and a short novel by Charles W. Diffin - Summary by Alan Winterrowd

title: The Dark Side of Healthcare
author: Heather Skousen
description:
The Dark Side of Healthcare

Healthcare is built on trust.

Patients trust providers with their lives. Families trust healthcare organizations to do the right thing. Communities trust the systems designed to protect them.

But what happens when those systems fail?

Hosted by Heather Skousen, MBA, a healthcare executive with more than 20 years of leadership experience and a Bachelor's degree in Criminal Justice, alongside Dena Diamond, a healthcare revenue cycle specialist with more than 18 years of experience in insurance benefits, prior authorization, and patient access, The Dark Side of Healthcare examines the stories that reveal the hidden vulnerabilities within healthcare.

Through investigative storytelling, case reviews, and candid discussion, we explore fraud, corruption, patient harm, abuse of power, regulatory failures, insurance barriers, healthcare policy, leadership breakdowns, and crimes that intersect with healthcare.

Some episodes involve healthcare-related murders and other criminal acts. But this is not a traditional true crime podcast. We are less interested in the perpetrator than we are in the system that allowed the event to occur.

What warning signs were missed?

What safeguards failed?

Who was responsible for oversight?

What organizational, financial, cultural, or regulatory factors created the conditions that made the tragedy possible?

Each episode brings together three distinct perspectives. Heather provides insight from healthcare leadership and operations. Dena offers expertise from the front lines of insurance, prior authorization, and revenue cycle management. Completing the panel is our resident true-crime enthusiast, Holly, who is intentionally not told the case beforehand. Hearing the details in real time alongside the audience, she asks the questions many listeners are already thinking and provides an authentic outside perspective on complex healthcare issues.

Together, we go beyond the headlines to examine not only what happened, but why it happened, what should have prevented it, and what lessons healthcare professionals, policymakers, patients, and organizations can learn from it.

Whether you work in healthcare, advocate for patients, study criminal justice, or simply want to better understand the system that impacts all of us, The Dark Side of Healthcare offers a deeper look at the stories most people never hear—and the lessons we cannot afford to ignore.

Fraud. Greed. Power. Harm.

Because the most important question isn't what happened. It's why it happened—and what should have stopped it.

title: Astounding Stories 05, May 1930
description: The fifth issue of Astounding Stories continues the Ray Cummings serial "Brigands of the Moon", begins a 4 part novel by Murray Leinster, and presents a sequel by Sewell Peaslee Wright to his story in the March issue. Also included are stories by Lilith Lorraine and Victor Rousseau. - Summary by Alan Winterrowd

title: Astounding Stories 01, January 1930
description: In January of 1930 a new magazine with a flashy color cover appeared on newsstands, Astounding Stories of Super-Science. Filled with stories of adventure, sometimes with only a tinge of science, this magazine was to host and nurture many science fiction giants like Murray Leinster and Ray Cummings and would help inspire many of the writers of the "Golden Age of Science Fiction". This inaugural issue includes stories by Murray Leinster, Ray Cummings, S. P. Meek, Victor Rousseau and others. (Summary by Alan Winterrowd)

title: Five Beloved Stories by O. Henry
description: O. Henry wrote over 600 short stories. Naturally I have my personal top 20 stories that just seem to stand out because of their form, writing style and ability to convey real personalities in a very few words. From these 20 I've chosen five that seem outstanding examples of the short story art form. Stories like The Gift of the Magi; The Cop and the Anthem; Man about Town; A Cosmopolite in a Cafe and Mammon and the Archer. So this is a collection of just five O.Henry stories that many people, including me, have loved and remembered over the years. And this time, thanks to LibriVox, I have the enormous pleasure of not just reading them to myself, but also the joy of reading them aloud! What fun, eh? (Summary by Phil Chenevert)

title: Futuria Fantasia, Spring 1940
description: A collection of short science fiction stories, edited by Ray Bradbury. Authors include Lyle Monroe, J. E. Kelleam, Hank Kuttner, J. H. Haggard, Ron Reynolds, Damon Knight, and Hannes V. Bok. (Summary by Lois Hill)

title: Arrival of Jimpson
description: This offering, "The Arrival of Jimpson, and Other Stories for Boys About Boys" contains eleven short stories by the prolific author of boys scholastic and collegiate sports and adventure novels. The stories include boys playing football, boys playing baseball, boys creating a checkers tournament, boys fishing on private property, a boys' hazing incident, a poor boy selling mittens to pay for his college room and board, and much more. A little something for everyone.... - Summary by Donald Cummings

title: Hungry Stones And Other Stories
description: This is a collection of short stories written by the Nobel laureate Rabindranath Tagore. The stories contained in this volume were translated by several hands. The version of The Victory is the author's own work. The seven stories which follow were translated by Mr. C. F. Andrews, with the help of the author's help. Assistance has also been given by the Rev. E. J. Thompson, Panna Lal Basu, Prabhat Kumar Mukerjii, and the Sister Nivedita.(Summary by sidhu177)

title: Fables of Phaedrus
description: The fable is a small narrative, in prose or verse, which has as its main characteristic the aim of conveying a moral lesson (the "moral"), implicitly or, more normally, explicitly expressed. Even though the modern concept of fable is that it should have animals or inanimate objects as characters - an idea supported by the works of famous fabulists such as Aesop and La Fontaine - Phaedrus, the most important Latin fabulist, is innovative in his writing. Although many of his fables do depict animals or objects assuming speech, he also has many short stories about men, writing narratives that seem to the modern eye more like short tales than fables.Despite many other fables being attributed to Phaedrus, only five books are considered by scholarship to have been written by him. Phaedrus' five books of fables are here presented in a translation to English prose by Henry Thomas Ridley. (Summary by Leni)

title: First Love and Other Fascinating Stories of Spanish Life
description: This is a collection of humorous short stories bound to entertain and make readers chuckle!

title: Heart of Happy Hollow
description: Happy Hollow; are you wondering where it is? Wherever Negroes colonise in the cities or villages, north or south, wherever the hod carrier, the porter, and the waiter are the society men of the town; wherever the picnic and the excursion are the chief summer diversion, and the revival the winter time of repentance, wherever the cheese cloth veil obtains at a wedding, and the little white hearse goes by with black mourners in the one carriage behind, there—there—is Happy Hollow. Wherever laughter and tears rub elbows day by day, and the spirit of labour and laziness shake hands, there—there—is Happy Hollow, and of some of it may the following pages show the heart. (Summary by the author)

title: Golden Fleece and the Heroes Who Lived Before Achilles
description: This is Irish folklorist Padraic Colum's masterful retelling of many Greek myths, focusing on Jason and the Argonauts' quest to find the Golden Fleece. He also includes the stories of Atalanta, Heracles, Perseus, Theseus, and others. (Summary by Elizabeth Klett) Note: Section 25 was replaced on June 2, 2020. The original file was missing the second portion of the text. The new file has the section in its entirety with a different reader for the second portion.

title: Jimmyjohn Boss and Other Stories
description: This is the fifth published book of Owen Wister, author of the archetypical Western novel, The Virginian. Published in 1900, it comprises eight Western short stories. (Summary by David Wales)

title: Members of the Family
description: Members of the Family is a collection of eight short stories about people in the Wyoming Territory in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. (Summary by David Wales)

title: Four Million (Version 2)
description: The man with the surprise endings: that was O. Henry's trademark. This is the second published collection of short stories by O. Henry originally released in 1906. There are twenty five stories of various lengths including several of his best known works such as "The Gift of the Magi" and "The Cop and the Anthem". The book's title refers to the then population of New York City where many of the stories are set. Some society snobs has the opinion that there were only 'Four Hundred' people in New York City who were really worth noticing. But O. Henry felt that every person in New York was worth noticing and had their own story which is explains the title of this collection little stories of the 'Four Million.'" To O. Henry, everyone in New York counted. And everyone had a story.

title: Paulownia: Seven Stories from Contemporary Japanese Writers
description: Paulownia is a collection of seven stories by three Japanese authors from the late 19th and early 20th century. Mori Ōgai was an army surgeon who was sent to study in Germany, where he developed an interest in Western literature. His most famous work is The Wild Geese (Gan). This collection contains his short stories Takase Bune, Hanako, and The Pier. Nagai Kafū's writings center mostly around the entertainment districts of Tokyo with their geisha and prostitutes. Here, his stories The bill-collecting and Ukiyo-e are presented. Shimazaki Tōson was one of the representatives of Japanese naturalism, which we can see in his stories A Domestic Animal and Tsugaru Strait. Taketomo Torao, the editor and translator of this volume, translated many Western works into Japanese, for example the Rubayat, the Divine Comedy, and works by Shakespeare. He was also a writer in his own right.

title: Cabbages and Kings
description: This work is O. Henry's first published volume and is considered to be his only novel. The plot is composed of several short stories, which were inspired by the author's six-month stay in Honduras in the late 1890s. "The incidents embracing as they do, a variety of subjects, hang loosely together, so loosely in fact, that at times one finds no apparent connection between them at all, and yet in the end one sees how each is intimately related to the other. ...Written by a less able hand than O. Henry's the book might have been a sad jumble, perhaps comprehensible to none but the Walrus--but as it is, one finds a joy in its every obscurity." -- The New York Times Book Review, Dec. 17, 1904) Great mysteries are ofen meta-mysteries. For over a century this novel has masqueraded as a collection of loosely connected short stories. Looking backwards through a prism (now a commonplace critical contortion) Cabbages and Kings foreshadows many shadings: "At Swim-Two-Birds", "Pierre Menard, Author of the Quixote", "Tyrants Destroyed", "The Continuity of Parks". -- Elliot Gage O. Henry spent his first years as a writer in Austin, Texas, and the University of Texas maintains a 219-item collection of his works at the Portal to Texas History (reference to publicly-available items which may not be in public domain offered to enthusiasts). — Eric W. Metzler

title: Waifs and Strays
description: These 12 O. Henry stories all deal with waifs and strays in one way or another; people who have somehow become adrift in the current of life. Will they find their way on their own or be helped by kind hearted folk or perhaps, stay a waif and stray, somehow outside the normal life of society? All naturally have the wonderful O. Henry beautiful way with words and people. So if you are in the mood to enjoy some sensuous sounds and convoluted flowing phrases unique to William Sydney Porter, give these a listen. And of course the endings cannot ever be predicted. Ever! (Summary by Phil Chenevert)

title: Great Stone Face and Other Tales of the White Mountains
description: A collection of four short stories by Nathaniel Hawthorne, the common theme of which is New Hampshire's White Mountains. Consists of: The Great Stone Face, written in 1850 and revolves around the 'Old Man of the Mountain (Cannon Mtn.) in New Hampshire which sadly collapsed on May 3, 2003; The Ambitious Guest, written in 1835; The Great Carbuncle, written in 1837; and Sketches From Memory, written sometime prior to The Great Carbuncle as will become obvious. (Summary by Roger Melin)

title: Four Million
description: Born in 1862 and died in 1910, O. Henry’s birth name is William Sydney Porter; however, he adopted the pen name O. Henry while in prison. He published 10 collections and over 600 short stories during his lifetime.The Four Million is the second book written by O. Henry while he served time for embezzlement in a penitentiary in Ohio. The book is a series of short stories which take place in New York City in the early years of the 20th century and are representative of the surprise endings that popularized O. Henry’s work. They also capture his use of coincidence or chance to create humor in the story. O Henry wrote about ordinary people in everyday circumstances. He is quoted as once saying, “There are stories in everything. I’ve got some of my best yarns from park benches, lampposts and newspaper stands.”I hope you enjoy the following readings as much as I enjoyed recording them.(Summary by Marian Brown)

title: Lord Arthur Savile's Crime and Other Stories
